title: Case Study: Chinook Salmon Bioenergetics
text: vignette walks complete bioenergetics analysis juvenile Oncorhynchus tshawytscha (Chinook salmon) using conditions representative interior Pacific Northwest lake single growing season (180 days, April–September). cover: Setting model built-parameter database Estimating daily consumption target final weight (binary search) Quantifying uncertainty via bootstrap resampling Summarising growth, energy budgets, feeding performance

title: Case Study: Chinook Salmon Bioenergetics
text: fb4package ships built-database (fish4_parameters) covering 105 parameterisations published literature.
code:
data(fish4_parameters)  chinook_db <- fish4_parameters[["Oncorhynchus tshawytscha"]] stage      <- if ("juvenile" %in% names(chinook_db$life_stages)) {                 "juvenile"               } else {                 names(chinook_db$life_stages)[1]               }  sp_params <- chinook_db$life_stages[[stage]] sp_info   <- chinook_db$species_info sp_info$life_stage <- stage  cat("Life stage  :", stage, "
") #> Life stage  : adult cat("CEQ (consumption equation):", sp_params$consumption$CEQ, "
") #> CEQ (consumption equation): 3 cat("REQ (respiration equation):", sp_params$respiration$REQ, "
") #> REQ (respiration equation): 1

title: Case Study: Chinook Salmon Bioenergetics
text: simulate 180-day seasonal temperature profile typical Pacific Northwest lake (April September), peak late July.
code: set.seed(42) days      <- 1:180 base_temp <- 7 + 7 * sin(pi * (days - 20) / 180)   # peak ~14 °C at day 110 temp_vec  <- pmax(2, base_temp + rnorm(180, 0, 0.4))  temp_data <- data.frame(   Day         = days,   Temperature = round(temp_vec, 2) )  cat(sprintf("Temperature range : %.1f – %.1f °C\n",             min(temp_data$Temperature), max(temp_data$Temperature))) #> Temperature range : 4.6 – 15.0 °C cat(sprintf("Mean temperature  : %.1f °C\n", mean(temp_data$Temperature))) #> Mean temperature  : 11.2 °C

title: Case Study: Chinook Salmon Bioenergetics
text: Juvenile Chinook Pacific Northwest lakes consume primarily forage fish (alewife) summer, supplemented shrimp invertebrates spring autumn. Prey energy densities typical values literature (J/g wet weight).
code: alewife <- pmax(0, 0.55 + 0.25 * sin(pi * (days - 30) / 180)) shrimp  <- pmax(0, 0.28 - 0.10 * sin(pi * (days - 30) / 180)) inverts <- pmax(0, 1 - alewife - shrimp) total   <- alewife + shrimp + inverts  diet_props <- data.frame(   Day     = days,   Alewife = round(alewife / total, 4),   Shrimp  = round(shrimp  / total, 4),   Inverts = round(inverts / total, 4) )  prey_energy <- data.frame(   Day     = days,   Alewife = 4900,   # J/g (wet weight)   Shrimp  = 3200,   Inverts = 2600 )  cat("Diet proportions (first 3 days):\n") #> Diet proportions (first 3 days): print(head(diet_props, 3)) #>   Day Alewife Shrimp Inverts #> 1   1  0.4288 0.3285  0.2427 #> 2   2  0.4326 0.3269  0.2404 #> 3   3  0.4365 0.3254  0.2381

title: Case Study: Chinook Salmon Bioenergetics
text: model components assembled single Bioenergetic object. Initial weight set 5 g, representing post-emergence juvenile.
code: bio_chinook <- Bioenergetic(   species_params     = sp_params,   species_info       = sp_info,   environmental_data = list(temperature = temp_data),   diet_data          = list(     proportions = diet_props,     prey_names  = c("Alewife", "Shrimp", "Inverts"),     energies    = prey_energy   ),   simulation_settings = list(initial_weight = 5, duration = 180) )  # Predator energy density: linear interpolation from 4 200 to 5 000 J/g # as the fish accumulate lipids through summer (PREDEDEQ = 1 from DB) bio_chinook$species_params$predator$ED_ini <- 4200 bio_chinook$species_params$predator$ED_end <- 5000  print(bio_chinook) #> FB4 Bioenergetic Model #> ========================= #> Species: Oncorhynchus tshawytscha (Chinook salmon (adult)) #> Setup: 5 g -> 180 days #>  #> Components: #>   [OK] Parameters: 41 params (consumption, respiration, activity, sda, egestion, excretion, predator, source, notes) #>   [OK] Temperature: 180 days (4.6-15°C) #>   [OK] Diet: 3 prey species, 180 days #>  #> Status: Ready for fitting

title: Case Study: Chinook Salmon Bioenergetics
text: Model setup dashboard: environmental diet data coverage. Seasonal temperature profile used simulation. Daily diet composition 180-day season.
code: plot(bio_chinook, type = "dashboard") plot(bio_chinook, type = "temperature", colors = "red") plot(bio_chinook, type = "diet", colors = "green")

title: Case Study: Chinook Salmon Bioenergetics
text: ask: feeding level (p) produces final weight 40 g 180 days? standard FB4 approach target weight measured field.
code:
res_bs <- run_fb4(   x         = bio_chinook,   fit_to    = "Weight",   fit_value = 40,   strategy  = "binary_search",   verbose   = FALSE )  cat(sprintf("Estimated p-value     : %.4f
", res_bs$summary$p_value)) cat(sprintf("Final weight          : %.1f g
", res_bs$summary$final_weight)) cat(sprintf("Total consumption     : %.1f g
", res_bs$summary$total_consumption_g)) cat(sprintf("Simulation converged  : %s
",     res_bs$summary$converged)) #> Estimated p-value     : 0.3714 #> Final weight          : 40.0 g #> Total consumption     : 141.2 g #> Simulation converged  : TRUE

title: Case Study: Chinook Salmon Bioenergetics
text: Daily weight trajectory binary search fit.
code: plot(res_bs, type = "growth")

text: Daily energy budget partitioning (J/day).
code: plot(res_bs, type = "energy")

code: plot(res_bs, type = "dashboard")

what: 6. Consumption with a fixed feeding level
title: Case Study: Chinook Salmon Bioenergetics
text: p known (e.g., bioenergetics study), use strategy = "direct_p_value" forward-simulate without fitting.
code: res_direct <- run_fb4(   x         = bio_chinook,   fit_to    = "p_value",   fit_value = 0.75,   strategy  = "direct_p_value",   verbose   = FALSE )  cat(sprintf("Final weight at p = 0.75 : %.1f g\n", res_direct$summary$final_weight)) cat(sprintf("Total consumption        : %.1f g\n", res_direct$summary$total_consumption_g)) #> Final weight at p = 0.75 : 376.0 g #> Total consumption        : 1055.3 g

title: Case Study: Chinook Salmon Bioenergetics
text: field data include multiple final weights (e.g., sample individually tagged fish), bootstrap resampling propagates measurement variability p estimate. simulate 25 observed final weights around binary-search result, CV 8 % mimic realistic field sampling variability. Bootstrap distribution estimated p-values 95% CI.
code:
set.seed(123) n_obs           <- 25 final_wt_true   <- res_bs$summary$final_weight obs_weights     <- rnorm(n_obs, mean = final_wt_true, sd = final_wt_true * 0.08)  res_boot <- run_fb4(   x                = bio_chinook,   fit_to           = "Weight",   observed_weights = obs_weights,   strategy         = "bootstrap",   n_bootstrap      = 100,   upper            = 1,   parallel         = FALSE,   confidence_level = 0.95,   verbose          = FALSE )  cat(sprintf("p mean (bootstrap) : %.4f
", res_boot$summary$p_mean)) cat(sprintf("p SD               : %.4f
", res_boot$summary$p_sd)) cat(sprintf("95%% CI             : [%.4f, %.4f]
",             res_boot$method_data$confidence_intervals$p_ci_lower,             res_boot$method_data$confidence_intervals$p_ci_upper)) plot(res_boot, type = "uncertainty")

title: Case Study: Chinook Salmon Bioenergetics
text: fb4package provides four dedicated analysis functions extract ecologically meaningful metrics fb4_result object.
code:
growth_stats   <- analyze_growth_patterns(res_bs) feeding_stats  <- analyze_feeding_performance(res_bs) energy_budget  <- analyze_energy_budget(res_bs)  # Growth metrics cat("=== Growth ===
") #> === Growth === cat(sprintf("  Final weight            : %.1f g
",             growth_stats$final_weight$estimate)) #>   Final weight            : 40.0 g cat(sprintf("  Total growth            : %.1f g
",             growth_stats$total_growth$estimate)) #>   Total growth            : 35.0 g cat(sprintf("  Specific growth rate    : %.4f g/g/day
",             growth_stats$specific_growth_rate$estimate)) #>   Specific growth rate    : 1.1553 g/g/day  # Feeding performance cat("
=== Feeding performance ===
") #>  #> === Feeding performance === cat(sprintf("  Total consumption       : %.1f g
",             feeding_stats$total_consumption$estimate)) #>   Total consumption       : 141.2 g cat(sprintf("  Gross conv. efficiency  : %.3f
",             feeding_stats$gross_conversion_efficiency$estimate))

title: Case Study: Chinook Salmon Bioenergetics
text: estimated p ≈ 0.62 indicates juvenile Chinook consumed approximately 62 % bioenergetically predicted maximum ration 180-day growing season. gross conversion efficiency near 0.13–0.15 consistent published values salmonids temperature ranges (Deslauriers et al. 2017). energy budget plot shows respiration dominates energy expenditure (~55–60 % consumed energy), egestion excretion accounting another 20 %. remaining ~20–25 % directed towards somatic growth.

title: Introduction to fb4package
text: fb4package implements Fish Bioenergetics 4.0 (Deslauriers et al. 2017) R. model allocates consumed energy among metabolism, waste, growth produce daily estimates fish consumption weight gain. Working package follows three-step pattern: build Bioenergetic object holds species parameters, temperature, diet, initial conditions; pass run_fb4() run simulation fit feeding level; extract plot results.

title: Introduction to fb4package
text: package ships built-database covering 105 models (73 species). Retrieve Chinook salmon (Oncorhynchus tshawytscha) inspect one life stage:
code:
data(fish4_parameters) cat("Species in database:", length(fish4_parameters), "
") #> Species in database: 73 head(names(fish4_parameters), 8) #> [1] "Alosa pseudoharengus"           "Gadus morhua"                   #> [3] "Brevoortia tyrannus"            "Thymallus arcticus baicalensis" #> [5] "Clupea harengus"                "Anchoa mitchilli"               #> [7] "Hypophthalmichthys nobilis"     "Coregonus hoyi" chinook_db  <- fish4_parameters[["Oncorhynchus tshawytscha"]] stage       <- names(chinook_db$life_stages)[1] cat("Life stage used:", stage, "
") #> Life stage used: adult  sp_params   <- chinook_db$life_stages[[stage]] sp_info     <- chinook_db$species_info  # Key consumption parameters cat("CEQ:", sp_params$consumption$CEQ,     "  CA:", sp_params$consumption$CA,     "  CB:", sp_params$consumption$CB, "
") #> CEQ: 3   CA: 0.303   CB: -0.275

title: Introduction to fb4package
text: Temperature diet supplied data frames Day column.
code: days <- 1:100  # Seasonal temperature (°C) temp_data <- data.frame(   Day         = days,   Temperature = round(10 + 5 * sin(2 * pi * (days - 60) / 365), 2) )  # Two-prey diet (constant proportions, J/g energy densities) diet_data <- list(   proportions = data.frame(Day = days, Alewife = 0.7, Shrimp = 0.3),   prey_names  = c("Alewife", "Shrimp"),   energies    = data.frame(Day = days, Alewife = 4900, Shrimp = 3200) )  head(temp_data, 3) #>   Day Temperature #> 1   1        5.75 #> 2   2        5.80 #> 3   3        5.84

title: Introduction to fb4package
code: bio <- Bioenergetic(   species_params      = sp_params,   species_info        = sp_info,   environmental_data  = list(temperature = temp_data),   diet_data           = diet_data,   simulation_settings = list(initial_weight = 1800, duration = 100) )  # Required when PREDEDEQ = 2 (energy density as function of weight) bio$species_params$predator$ED_ini <- 4500 bio$species_params$predator$ED_end <- 5500  print(bio) #> FB4 Bioenergetic Model #> ========================= #> Species: Oncorhynchus tshawytscha (Chinook salmon (adult)) #> Setup: 1800 g -> 100 days #>  #> Components: #>   [OK] Parameters: 41 params (consumption, respiration, activity, sda, egestion, excretion, predator, source, notes) #>   [OK] Temperature: 100 days (5.8-13.2°C) #>   [OK] Diet: 2 prey species, 100 days #>  #> Status: Ready for fitting

title: Introduction to fb4package
text: Run model constant proportion maximum consumption (p = 0.8):
code: res_direct <- run_fb4(bio, strategy = "direct", p_value = 0.8) print(res_direct) #> FB4 Simulation Results #> ========================= #> Species: Oncorhynchus tshawytscha #> Method: Direct Execution #> Duration: 100 days #>  #> RESULTS: #>   Initial weight: 1800 g #>   Final weight: 2647.37 g #>   Growth: 47.1% #>   Total consumption: 4821.23 g #>   P_value: 0.8 #>  #> FITTING: [OK] Successful

what: 4b. Binary search — fit to a target final weight
title: Introduction to fb4package
text: Find p produces target weight 3 000 g 100 days:
code:
res_bs <- run_fb4(bio,                   strategy  = "binary_search",                   fit_to    = "Weight",                   fit_value = 3000)  cat("Estimated p-value :", round(res_bs$summary$p_value, 4), "
") cat("Final weight      :", round(res_bs$summary$final_weight, 1), "g
") cat("Total consumption :", round(res_bs$summary$total_consumption_g, 1), "g
") #> Estimated p-value : 0.9095 #> Final weight      : 3000 g #> Total consumption : 5771.2 g

what: 4c. MLE — uncertainty on p from observed weights
title: Introduction to fb4package
text: multiple fish weights available, strategy = "mle" returns point estimate p together standard error 95% confidence interval:
code:
set.seed(42) obs_weights <- rnorm(30, mean = 3000, sd = 80)   # 30 observed final weights  res_mle <- run_fb4(bio,                    strategy         = "mle",                    fit_to           = "Weight",                    observed_weights = obs_weights)  cat("p-value (MLE)  :", round(res_mle$summary$p_value, 4), "
") cat("SE             :", round(res_mle$summary$p_se,    4), "
") cat("Converged      :", res_mle$summary$converged, "
")

title: Introduction to fb4package
code: set.seed(42) res_boot <- run_fb4(bio,                     strategy         = "bootstrap",                     fit_to           = "Weight",                     observed_weights = obs_weights,                     upper            = 1,                     n_bootstrap      = 100,                     parallel         = FALSE)  cat("p mean (bootstrap):", round(res_boot$summary$p_mean, 4), "\n") cat("p SD              :", round(res_boot$summary$p_sd,   4), "\n")

title: Introduction to fb4package
text: three methods converge similar p estimates data — binary search gives point estimate, MLE bootstrap additionally quantify uncertainty: strategies use bio object (Chinook salmon, 100-day simulation). MLE bootstrap fit obs_weights; binary search fits target weight.

",             growth$specific_growth_rate$estimate)) #> Specific growth rate      : 0.5108 g/g/day cat(sprintf("Total consumption         : %.1f g

",             feeding$gross_conversion_efficiency$estimate))

title: Introduction to fb4package
text: following modules implemented standalone functions yet integrated main run_fb4() loop. can called directly custom analyses connected automatically future release.

title: Introduction to fb4package
text: Three bioaccumulation models (dietary uptake, gill exchange, bioenergetics-based): calculate_contaminant_accumulation() — daily bioaccumulation process_contaminant_params() — parameter pre-processing

title: Introduction to fb4package
text: Daily nitrogen phosphorus fluxes (ingestion, retention, excretion): calculate_nutrient_balance() — daily N/P budget calculate_nutrient_efficiencies() — N P use efficiencies process_nutrient_params() — parameter pre-processing

title: Introduction to fb4package
text: Natural, fishing, predation mortality rates can processed via process_mortality_params() computed step--step calculate_mortality_reproduction(), mortality rates yet applied automatically inside run_fb4(). Note: spawning energy loss (reproductive cost) already integrated applies automatically reproduction_data supplied Bioenergetic object.

title: Introduction to fb4package
text: Deslauriers D, Chipps SR, Breck JE, Rice JA, Madenjian CP (2017). Fish Bioenergetics 4.0: R-Based Modeling Application. Fisheries 42(11):586–596. https://doi.org/10.1080/03632415.2017.1377558

title: Validation against FB4-Shiny
text: article verifies fb4package produces results numerically identical original FB4-Shiny application (Deslauriers et al. 2017, v1.1.7) across five simulation modes across nutrient contaminant sub-models. reference implementation consists exact arithmetic server.R FB4-Shiny, adapted accept R objects instead CSV uploads. implementations receive identical inputs: species parameters (pulled fish4_parameters), temperature series, diet, initial conditions.

title: Validation against FB4-Shiny
code: data(fish4_parameters) chinook_key <- grep("tshawytscha", names(fish4_parameters), value = TRUE)[1] db    <- fish4_parameters[[chinook_key]] stage <- if ("adult" %in% names(db$life_stages)) "adult" else names(db$life_stages)[1] sp    <- db$life_stages[[stage]]  cat("Species:", chinook_key, "| Stage:", stage, "\n") #> Species: Oncorhynchus tshawytscha | Stage: adult cat("CEQ:", sp$consumption$CEQ,     " CA:", sp$consumption$CA,     " CB:", sp$consumption$CB, "\n") #> CEQ: 3  CA: 0.303  CB: -0.275

title: Validation against FB4-Shiny
code: gp <- function(cat, nm) {   v <- sp[[cat]][[nm]]   if (is.null(v)) NA_real_ else as.numeric(v) }  CEQ <- gp("consumption","CEQ"); CA  <- gp("consumption","CA") CB  <- gp("consumption","CB");  CQ  <- gp("consumption","CQ") CTO <- gp("consumption","CTO"); CTM <- gp("consumption","CTM") CTL <- gp("consumption","CTL"); CK1 <- gp("consumption","CK1") CK4 <- gp("consumption","CK4")  REQ <- gp("respiration","REQ"); RA  <- gp("respiration","RA") RB  <- gp("respiration","RB");  RQ  <- gp("respiration","RQ") RTO <- gp("respiration","RTO"); RTM <- gp("respiration","RTM") RTL <- gp("respiration","RTL"); RK1 <- gp("respiration","RK1") RK4 <- gp("respiration","RK4"); RK5 <- gp("respiration","RK5")  ACT  <- gp("activity","ACT");  BACT <- gp("activity","BACT") SDA  <- gp("sda","SDA")  EGEQ <- gp("egestion","EGEQ"); FA <- gp("egestion","FA") FB_  <- gp("egestion","FB");   FG <- gp("egestion","FG") EXEQ <- gp("excretion","EXEQ"); UA <- gp("excretion","UA") UB   <- gp("excretion","UB");  UG <- gp("excretion","UG")  PREDEDEQ <- gp("predator","PREDEDEQ")  Oxycal <- 13560

title: Validation against FB4-Shiny
Validation against FB4-Shiny
code: set.seed(123) N_DIAS  <- 180 Init_W  <- 500     # g p_fixed <- 0.4 ED_ini  <- 4200; ED_fin <- 4600  days     <- 1:N_DIAS base_T   <- 10 + 4*sin(2*pi*(days-30)/N_DIAS) Temp_vec <- round(pmax(2, base_T + rnorm(N_DIAS, 0, 0.3)), 2)  prey_prop_mat <- matrix(c(rep(0.7, N_DIAS), rep(0.3, N_DIAS)),                         nrow = N_DIAS,                         dimnames = list(NULL, c("Alewife","Inverts"))) prey_ED_mat   <- matrix(c(rep(4900, N_DIAS), rep(2600, N_DIAS)),                         nrow = N_DIAS,                         dimnames = list(NULL, c("Alewife","Inverts"))) ind_prey_mat  <- matrix(0, nrow = N_DIAS, ncol = 2,                         dimnames = list(NULL, c("Alewife","Inverts"))) Pred_E_vec    <- seq(ED_ini, ED_fin, length.out = N_DIAS + 1)  cat(sprintf("%d days | W0 = %.0f g | p = %.2f | T = %.1f–%.1f °C\n",             N_DIAS, Init_W, p_fixed, min(Temp_vec), max(Temp_vec))) #> 180 days | W0 = 500 g | p = 0.40 | T = 5.6–14.6 °C

title: Validation against FB4-Shiny
text: functions reproduce arithmetic server.R verbatim.
code: # --- Temperature functions --- Cf1T <- function(T) exp(CQ*T) Cf2T <- function(T) {   if (T < CTM) { V <- (CTM-T)/(CTM-CTO); ft <- V^CX*exp(CX*(1-V)) } else ft <- 0   max(ft, 0) } Cf3T <- function(T) {   L1 <- exp(CG1*(T-CQ)); KA <- (CK1*L1)/(1+CK1*(L1-1))   L2 <- exp(CG2*(CTL-T)); KB <- (CK4*L2)/(1+CK4*(L2-1))   KA*KB } Cf4T <- function(T) max(exp(CQ*T + CK1*T^2 + CK4*T^3), 0)  Rf1T    <- function(T) exp(RQ*T) RACTf1T <- function(W,T) {   VEL <- if (T <= RTL) ACT*W^RK4*exp(BACT*T) else RK1*W^RK4*exp(RK5*T)   exp(RTO*VEL) } Rf2T <- function(T) {   if (T < RTM) { V <- (RTM-T)/(RTM-RTO); ft <- V^RX*exp(RX*(1-V)) } else ft <- 1e-6   max(ft, 1e-6) }  # --- Bioenergetic functions --- consumo_fn <- function(T, W, p) {   Cmax <- CA*W^CB   ft   <- switch(as.character(CEQ),                  "1" = Cf1T(T), "2" = Cf2T(T),                  "3" = Cf3T(T), "4" = Cf4T(T))   Cmax*p*ft }  respiracion_fn <- function(T, W) {   Rmax <- RA*W^RB   if (REQ == 1) { ft <- Rf1T(T); ACT_v <- RACTf1T(W,T) } else { ft <- Rf2T(T); ACT_v <- ACT }   Rmax*ft*ACT_v }  egestion_fn <- function(C, T, p, i) {   switch(as.character(EGEQ),     "1" = FA*C,     "2" = FA*(T^FB_)*exp(FG*p)*C,     "3" = { PE  <- FA*(T^FB_)*exp(FG*p)             PFF <- sum(ind_prey_mat[i,]*prey_prop_mat[i,])             PF  <- ((PE-0.1)/0.9)*(1-PFF)+PFF; PF*C },     "4" = FA*(T^FB_)*C) }  excrecion_fn <- function(C, Eg, T, p) {   switch(as.character(EXEQ),     "1" = UA*(C-Eg),     "2" =, "3" = UA*(T^UB)*exp(UG*p)*(C-Eg),     "4" = UA*(T^UB)*(C-Eg)) }  # --- Daily growth loop --- grow_shiny_daily <- function(Temperature, W, mode = "p_value", mode_value = 0.4,                              prey_pm, prey_em, Fin) {   out <- vector("list", Fin)   for (i in seq_len(Fin)) {     Pred_E_i  <- Pred_E_vec[min(i,   length(Pred_E_vec))]     Pred_E_ip1 <- Pred_E_vec[min(i+1, length(Pred_E_vec))]     mean_prey <- sum(prey_pm[i,] * prey_em[i,])      if (mode == "p_value") {       p       <- mode_value       Cons_gg <- consumo_fn(Temperature[i], W, p)     } else if (mode == "Ration") {       Cons_gg <- mode_value       Cmax    <- consumo_fn(Temperature[i], W, 1.0)       p       <- if (Cmax > 0) Cons_gg/Cmax else 0     } else if (mode == "Ration_prey") {       Cons_gg <- mode_value / W       Cmax    <- consumo_fn(Temperature[i], W, 1.0)       p       <- if (Cmax > 0) Cons_gg/Cmax else 0     }      Cons <- Cons_gg * mean_prey     Eg   <- egestion_fn(Cons, Temperature[i], p, i)     Ex   <- excrecion_fn(Cons, Eg, Temperature[i], p)     S    <- SDA*(Cons-Eg)     Res  <- respiracion_fn(Temperature[i], W)*Oxycal      G      <- Cons-(Res+Eg+Ex+S)     egain  <- G*W     finalwt <- (egain + Pred_E_i*W) / Pred_E_ip1     if (is.na(finalwt) || finalwt < 0) finalwt <- 0      out[[i]] <- list(       Day                  = i,       Starting_Weight      = W,       Weight               = finalwt,       Weight_change        = finalwt - W,       Temperature          = Temperature[i],       Mean_Prey_Energy_J_g = mean_prey,       Consumption_gg       = Cons_gg,       Consumption_energy   = Cons,       Respiration          = Res,       Egestion             = Eg,       Excretion            = Ex,       SDA                  = S,       Net_energy           = G,       Energy_density       = Pred_E_ip1,       Cons_Alewife_g       = Cons_gg*W*prey_pm[i,"Alewife"],       Cons_Inverts_g       = Cons_gg*W*prey_pm[i,"Inverts"]     )     W <- finalwt   }   as.data.frame(do.call(rbind, lapply(out, as.data.frame))) }  # --- Binary search (Weight or Consumption target) --- fit_p_shiny <- function(IW, target, fit_type, Temperature,                         prey_pm, prey_em, Fin,                         W_tol = 0.001, max_iter = 100) {   p_min <- 0; p_max <- 5; p <- 0.5    sim_val <- function(p_try) {     df <- grow_shiny_daily(Temperature, IW, "p_value", p_try, prey_pm, prey_em, Fin)     if (fit_type == "Weight") tail(df$Weight, 1)     else sum(df$Consumption_gg * df$Starting_Weight)   }    val <- sim_val(p)   for (iter in seq_len(max_iter)) {     if (abs(val - target) <= W_tol) break     if (val > target) p_max <- p else p_min <- p     p <- (p_min + p_max)/2     val <- sim_val(p)   }   list(p = p, converged = (abs(val-target) <= W_tol), final_val = val) }

title: Validation against FB4-Shiny
code: compare_outputs <- function(shiny_df, pkg_df, method_label,   cols = c("Starting_Weight","Weight","Weight_change",            "Mean_Prey_Energy_J_g","Consumption_gg","Consumption_energy",            "Respiration","Egestion","Excretion","SDA",            "Net_energy","Energy_density","Cons_Alewife_g","Cons_Inverts_g")) {    cols_ok <- intersect(cols, intersect(names(shiny_df), names(pkg_df)))    res <- lapply(cols_ok, function(col) {     s <- as.numeric(shiny_df[[col]])     p <- as.numeric(pkg_df[[col]])     if (all(is.na(s)) || all(is.na(p))) return(NULL)     d    <- p - s     rmse <- sqrt(mean(d^2, na.rm = TRUE))     r2   <- if (var(s, na.rm=TRUE) > 0) cor(s, p, use="complete.obs")^2 else NA     data.frame(method = method_label, col = col,                max_abs_diff = max(abs(d), na.rm=TRUE),                rmse = rmse, r2 = r2)   })   do.call(rbind, Filter(Negate(is.null), res)) }

title: Validation against FB4-Shiny
text: Worst-case RMSE per method (core bioenergetic variables) RMSE values floating-point precision (~1e-13) direct methods. Binary-search methods show slightly larger discrepancies (~1e-4 Weight, ~5e-3 Consumption) reflect convergence tolerance, algorithmic differences.
code: all_metrics <- rbind(m1, m2, m3, m4, m5)  # Worst RMSE per method (excluding Energy_density — known reporting offset) core_cols <- setdiff(unique(all_metrics$col), "Energy_density") worst <- do.call(rbind, lapply(split(all_metrics[all_metrics$col %in% core_cols, ],                                      all_metrics[all_metrics$col %in% core_cols, "method"]),                                function(x) x[which.max(x$rmse), ]))  knitr::kable(   worst[, c("method", "col", "max_abs_diff", "rmse", "r2")],   digits   = c(0, 0, 2e-10, 2e-10, 8),   format.args = list(scientific = TRUE),   col.names   = c("Method", "Worst column", "Max |diff|", "RMSE", "R²"),   caption     = "Worst-case RMSE per method (core bioenergetic variables)" )

title: Validation against FB4-Shiny
text: systematic offset ~2.2 J/g exists Energy_density across methods. reporting convention difference, calculation error: package reports energy density start day (growth), FB4-Shiny reports end day. internal calculations use start--day energy density consistently.

title: Validation against FB4-Shiny
text: nutrient sub-model validated mass balance rather FB4-Shiny (implement nutrients). day: Nconsumed=Ngrowth+Nexcretion+NegestionN_{\text{consumed}} = N_{\text{growth}} + N_{\text{excretion}} + N_{\text{egestion}} Nutrient mass balance checks Daily nitrogen phosphorus fluxes 180 days.
code: prey_n <- data.frame(Day = c(1,N_DIAS), Alewife = c(0.025,0.025), Inverts = c(0.018,0.018)) prey_p <- data.frame(Day = c(1,N_DIAS), Alewife = c(0.004,0.004), Inverts = c(0.003,0.003)) pred_n <- data.frame(Day = c(1,N_DIAS), value = c(0.030,0.030)) pred_p <- data.frame(Day = c(1,N_DIAS), value = c(0.004,0.004)) n_ae   <- data.frame(Day = c(1,N_DIAS), Alewife = c(0.80,0.80), Inverts = c(0.75,0.75)) p_ae   <- data.frame(Day = c(1,N_DIAS), Alewife = c(0.60,0.60), Inverts = c(0.55,0.55))  bio_nut <- Bioenergetic(   species_params      = sp_pkg,   species_info        = list(scientific_name = "Oncorhynchus tshawytscha"),   environmental_data  = list(temperature = temp_df),   diet_data           = list(proportions = diet_df, prey_names = c("Alewife","Inverts"),                              energies = energy_df),   nutrient_data       = list(prey_n_concentrations    = prey_n,                              prey_p_concentrations    = prey_p,                              predator_n_concentration = pred_n,                              predator_p_concentration = pred_p,                              n_assimilation_efficiency = n_ae,                              p_assimilation_efficiency = p_ae),   simulation_settings = list(initial_weight = Init_W, duration = N_DIAS) )  nut <- run_fb4(bio_nut, fit_to = "p_value", fit_value = p_fixed,                strategy = "direct", verbose = FALSE)$daily_output  N_err <- with(nut, abs(Nitrogen_consumed_g -                        (Nitrogen_growth_g + Nitrogen_excretion_g + Nitrogen_egestion_g))) P_err <- with(nut, abs(Phosphorus_consumed_g -                        (Phosphorus_growth_g + Phosphorus_excretion_g + Phosphorus_egestion_g)))  data.frame(   Check   = c("N mass balance (max error, g)", "P mass balance (max error, g)",               "N consumed ≥ 0", "P consumed ≥ 0"),   Result  = c(format(max(N_err, na.rm=TRUE), scientific=TRUE),               format(max(P_err, na.rm=TRUE), scientific=TRUE),               all(nut$Nitrogen_consumed_g   >= 0, na.rm=TRUE),               all(nut$Phosphorus_consumed_g >= 0, na.rm=TRUE)) ) |> knitr::kable(col.names = c("Check", "Result"),                   caption = "Nutrient mass balance checks") par(mfrow = c(1, 2)) plot(nut$Day, nut$Nitrogen_consumed_g, type = "l", col = "steelblue",      xlab = "Day", ylab = "g N / day", main = "Nitrogen fluxes") lines(nut$Day, nut$Nitrogen_growth_g,    col = "darkgreen") lines(nut$Day, nut$Nitrogen_excretion_g, col = "firebrick", lty = 2) lines(nut$Day, nut$Nitrogen_egestion_g,  col = "orange",    lty = 2) legend("topright", legend = c("Consumed","Growth","Excretion","Egestion"),        col = c("steelblue","darkgreen","firebrick","orange"),        lty = c(1,1,2,2), bty = "n", cex = 0.8)  plot(nut$Day, nut$Phosphorus_consumed_g, type = "l", col = "steelblue",      xlab = "Day", ylab = "g P / day", main = "Phosphorus fluxes") lines(nut$Day, nut$Phosphorus_growth_g,    col = "darkgreen") lines(nut$Day, nut$Phosphorus_excretion_g, col = "firebrick", lty = 2) lines(nut$Day, nut$Phosphorus_egestion_g,  col = "orange",    lty = 2) par(mfrow = c(1, 1))

title: Validation against FB4-Shiny
text: Three CONTEQ equations tested. Checks: columns present, burden ≥ 0, model-specific behaviour (e.g. CONTEQ 1 zero clearance). Contaminant sub-model checks Contaminant body burden 180 days three CONTEQ equations.
code: cont_prey <- data.frame(Day=c(1,N_DIAS), Alewife=c(0.05,0.05), Inverts=c(0.02,0.02)) cont_ae   <- data.frame(Day=c(1,N_DIAS), Alewife=c(0.80,0.80), Inverts=c(0.70,0.70)) cont_te   <- data.frame(Day=c(1,N_DIAS), Alewife=c(0.85,0.85), Inverts=c(0.75,0.75))  run_conteq <- function(CONTEQ_num, extra = list()) {   cd <- c(list(CONTEQ = CONTEQ_num, initial_concentration = 0.10,                prey_concentrations = cont_prey,                transfer_efficiency = cont_te,                assimilation_efficiency = cont_ae), extra)   b <- Bioenergetic(     species_params      = sp_pkg,     species_info        = list(scientific_name = "Oncorhynchus tshawytscha"),     environmental_data  = list(temperature = temp_df),     diet_data           = list(proportions = diet_df, prey_names = c("Alewife","Inverts"),                                energies = energy_df),     contaminant_data    = cd,     simulation_settings = list(initial_weight = Init_W, duration = N_DIAS)   )   run_fb4(b, fit_to = "p_value", fit_value = p_fixed,           strategy = "direct", verbose = FALSE)$daily_output }  df_c1 <- run_conteq(1) df_c2 <- run_conteq(2) df_c3 <- run_conteq(3, list(gill_efficiency = 0.5, fish_water_partition = 5000,                              water_concentration = 0.0001, dissolved_fraction = 0.9,                              do_saturation = 0.9))  cont_checks <- data.frame(   Check = c(     "CONTEQ 1 — burden ≥ 0",     "CONTEQ 1 — clearance = 0 (pure accumulation)",     "CONTEQ 1 — burden monotone",     "CONTEQ 2 — burden ≥ 0",     "CONTEQ 2 — elimination active (>50% of days)",     "CONTEQ 3 — burden ≥ 0",     "CONTEQ 3 — elimination active (>50% of days)"   ),   Pass = c(     all(df_c1$Contaminant_burden_ug >= 0, na.rm=TRUE),     all(df_c1$Contaminant_clearance_ug_d == 0, na.rm=TRUE),     all(diff(df_c1$Contaminant_burden_ug) >= -1e-10, na.rm=TRUE),     all(df_c2$Contaminant_burden_ug >= 0, na.rm=TRUE),     mean(df_c2$Contaminant_clearance_ug_d > 0, na.rm=TRUE) > 0.5,     all(df_c3$Contaminant_burden_ug >= 0, na.rm=TRUE),     mean(df_c3$Contaminant_clearance_ug_d > 0, na.rm=TRUE) > 0.5   ) )  knitr::kable(cont_checks, caption = "Contaminant sub-model checks") plot(df_c1$Day, df_c1$Contaminant_burden_ug, type = "l",      col = "firebrick", lwd = 2,      xlab = "Day", ylab = "Burden (μg)", main = "Contaminant body burden by CONTEQ") lines(df_c2$Day, df_c2$Contaminant_burden_ug, col = "steelblue", lwd = 2) lines(df_c3$Day, df_c3$Contaminant_burden_ug, col = "darkgreen", lwd = 2) legend("topleft", legend = c("CONTEQ 1 (no clearance)",                              "CONTEQ 2 (T/W-dependent)",                              "CONTEQ 3 (Arnot & Gobas)"),        col = c("firebrick","steelblue","darkgreen"), lwd = 2, bty = "n")

title: Validation against FB4-Shiny
text: Across five simulation modes 180 daily time steps, fb4package reproduces FB4-Shiny within floating-point precision direct methods (RMSE < 10⁻¹²) within binary-search convergence tolerance iterative methods (RMSE < 10⁻⁴). nutrient sub-model closes N P mass balance < 10⁻⁸ g per day. three CONTEQ contaminant equations produce non-negative burdens expected elimination behaviour. fb4package can therefore used fully reproducible, scriptable replacement FB4-Shiny.

fish4_parameters[["Species name"]] ├── species_info        # taxonomic information ├── life_stages         # named list of life-stage parameter sets │   ├── juvenile        # (or adult, larval, etc.) │   │   ├── consumption │   │   ├── respiration │   │   ├── activity │   │   ├── sda │   │   ├── egestion │   │   ├── excretion │   │   └── predator    # energy density equation │   └── adult           # (if present) └── sources             # literature references # Inspect a single entry chinook <- fish4_parameters[["Oncorhynchus tshawytscha"]]  cat("=== Species info ===
") #> === Species info === print(chinook$species_info) #> $scientific_name #> [1] "Oncorhynchus tshawytscha" #>  #> $common_name #> [1] "Chinook salmon (adult)" #>  #> $family #> [1] "Salmonidae" #>  #> $order #> [1] "Salmoniformes"  cat("
=== Life stages available ===
") #>  #> === Life stages available === print(names(chinook$life_stages)) #> [1] "adult"  cat("
=== Sources ===
") #>  #> === Sources === print(chinook$sources) #> [1] "Stewart & Ibarra 1991; Plumb & Moffit 2015"

title: Exploring the Species Parameter Database
text: maximum consumption rate governed : Cmax=CA⋅WCB⋅f(T)C_{\max} = C_A \cdot W^{C_B} \cdot f(T) CA scales intercept CB weight exponent. Higher CA values indicate higher maximum ration relative body weight. Top 10 entries CA (maximum consumption coefficient)
code: # Extract CA, CB, CEQ for all entries (first life stage) param_table <- do.call(rbind, lapply(names(fish4_parameters), function(sp) {   entry <- fish4_parameters[[sp]]   stage <- names(entry$life_stages)[1]   cons  <- entry$life_stages[[stage]]$consumption    data.frame(     Species    = sp,     Stage      = stage,     CEQ        = cons$CEQ  %||% NA,     CA         = cons$CA   %||% NA,     CB         = cons$CB   %||% NA,     CTO        = cons$CTO  %||% NA,     CTM        = cons$CTM  %||% NA,     stringsAsFactors = FALSE   ) }))  # Show top 10 by CA (highest maximum ration) param_table_sorted <- param_table[order(-param_table$CA, na.last = TRUE), ] knitr::kable(   head(param_table_sorted[, c("Species", "Stage", "CEQ", "CA", "CB", "CTO", "CTM")], 10),   caption = "Top 10 entries by CA (maximum consumption coefficient)",   digits  = 4 )

title: Exploring the Species Parameter Database
text: Distribution consumption temperature optima (CTO) across database entries.
code: cto_vals <- na.omit(param_table$CTO)  hist(cto_vals,      breaks = 15,      main   = "Consumption temperature optima (CTO)",      xlab   = "CTO (°C)",      ylab   = "Number of entries",      col    = "steelblue",      border = "white") abline(v = median(cto_vals), col = "tomato", lwd = 2, lty = 2) legend("topright", legend = paste("Median =", round(median(cto_vals), 1), "°C"),        col = "tomato", lty = 2, lwd = 2, bty = "n")

title: Exploring the Species Parameter Database
text: database includes three equations predator (fish) energy density: PREDEDEQ = 1 (common), user must supply ED_ini ED_end (full ED_data vector) since daily energy density varies season condition factor. See Introduction vignette example.
code: prededeq_vals <- sapply(names(fish4_parameters), function(sp) {   entry <- fish4_parameters[[sp]]   stage <- names(entry$life_stages)[1]   entry$life_stages[[stage]]$predator$PREDEDEQ %||% NA })  cat("Distribution of PREDEDEQ across database:\n") #> Distribution of PREDEDEQ across database: print(table(prededeq_vals, useNA = "ifany")) #> prededeq_vals #>  1  2  3  #> 61 11  1

what: 7. Building a model directly from the database
title: Exploring the Species Parameter Database
code: # Retrieve brown trout if available, otherwise fall back to first salmonid target_sp <- grep("Salmo trutta|brown trout",                   names(fish4_parameters),                   value = TRUE, ignore.case = TRUE)  if (length(target_sp) == 0) {   target_sp <- onco_keys[1]   # Fall back to first Oncorhynchus } target_sp <- target_sp[1] cat("Using species:", target_sp, "\n") #> Using species: Salmo trutta  entry  <- fish4_parameters[[target_sp]] stage  <- names(entry$life_stages)[1] params <- entry$life_stages[[stage]] info   <- entry$species_info info$life_stage <- stage  # Minimal 60-day setup days_short <- 1:60 bio_db <- Bioenergetic(   species_params     = params,   species_info       = info,   environmental_data = list(     temperature = data.frame(Day = days_short,                              Temperature = 10 + 3 * sin(pi * days_short / 60))   ),   diet_data = list(     proportions = data.frame(Day = days_short, Invertebrates = 1),     prey_names  = "Invertebrates",     energies    = data.frame(Day = days_short, Invertebrates = 2800)   ),   simulation_settings = list(initial_weight = 50, duration = 60) )  # Supply energy density bounds for PREDEDEQ = 1 bio_db$species_params$predator$ED_ini <- 4000 bio_db$species_params$predator$ED_end <- 4500  res_db <- run_fb4(bio_db,                   fit_to    = "Weight",                   fit_value = 80,                   strategy  = "binary_search",                   verbose   = FALSE)  cat(sprintf("Estimated p : %.4f  |  Final weight : %.1f g\n",             res_db$summary$p_value,             res_db$summary$final_weight)) #> Estimated p : 0.6651  |  Final weight : 80.0 g

title: Statistical estimation strategies
text: run_fb4() supports four strategies estimating proportion maximum consumption (p): Every strategy takes Bioenergetic object input returns fb4_result object structure.

title: Statistical estimation strategies
text: Bioenergetic object used four strategies. represents adult Chinook salmon cohort (Oncorhynchus tshawytscha) 100-day growing season. Observed final weights simulated around target 3 000 g used likelihood bootstrap strategies:
code: data(fish4_parameters)  chinook_db <- fish4_parameters[["Oncorhynchus tshawytscha"]] stage      <- names(chinook_db$life_stages)[1] sp_params  <- chinook_db$life_stages[[stage]] sp_info    <- chinook_db$species_info  days <- 1:100 temp_data <- data.frame(   Day         = days,   Temperature = round(10 + 5 * sin(2 * pi * (days - 60) / 365), 2) ) diet_data <- list(   proportions = data.frame(Day = days, Alewife = 0.7, Shrimp = 0.3),   prey_names  = c("Alewife", "Shrimp"),   energies    = data.frame(Day = days, Alewife = 4900, Shrimp = 3200) )  bio <- Bioenergetic(   species_params      = sp_params,   species_info        = sp_info,   environmental_data  = list(temperature = temp_data),   diet_data           = diet_data,   simulation_settings = list(initial_weight = 1800, duration = 100) ) bio$species_params$predator$ED_ini <- 4500 bio$species_params$predator$ED_end <- 5500 set.seed(42) obs_weights <- rnorm(30, mean = 3000, sd = 80)

title: Statistical estimation strategies
text: Binary search finds p produces specified target weight. Use point estimate enough known growth target.
code: res_bs <- run_fb4(bio,                   strategy  = "binary_search",                   fit_to    = "Weight",                   fit_value = 3000)  cat("p estimate  :", round(res_bs$summary$p_value, 4), "\n") cat("Final weight:", round(res_bs$summary$final_weight, 1), "g\n")

title: Statistical estimation strategies
text: MLE finds p maximises likelihood observing supplied weights, assuming log-normal distribution around predicted weight. returns standard error 95 % confidence interval via profile likelihood.
code:
res_mle <- run_fb4(bio,                    strategy         = "mle",                    fit_to           = "Weight",                    observed_weights = obs_weights)  cat("p estimate :", round(res_mle$summary$p_value, 4), "
") cat("SE         :", round(res_mle$summary$p_se,    4), "
") cat("Converged  :", res_mle$summary$converged, "
")

title: Statistical estimation strategies
text: Bootstrap resamples observed weights re-fits model replicate, producing distribution p estimates without assuming particular form weight distribution.
code: res_boot <- run_fb4(bio,                     strategy         = "bootstrap",                     fit_to           = "Weight",                     observed_weights = obs_weights,                     n_bootstrap      = 100,                     upper            = 1,                     parallel         = FALSE)  cat("p mean :", round(res_boot$summary$p_mean, 4), "\n") cat("p SD   :", round(res_boot$summary$p_sd,   4), "\n")

title: Statistical estimation strategies
text: hierarchical strategy designed mark-recapture bioenergetics: individual fish tagged, weighed release (initial weight), re-weighed recapture (final weight) fixed monitoring period. model estimates p value individual, pools estimates population-level distribution feeding levels. answers two biological questions: - average feeding level (p) population observed environmental conditions? - variable individual feeding behaviour within population?

title: Statistical estimation strategies
text: First use binary search find p consistent bio object (initial weight 1 800 g, 100 days). value becomes centre simulated population distribution.
code: res_ref <- run_fb4(bio,                    strategy  = "binary_search",                    fit_to    = "Weight",                    fit_value = 2500) ref_p <- res_ref$summary$p_value cat("Reference p (target 2 500 g):", round(ref_p, 4), "\n") set.seed(123) n_fish <- 20  # Cohort: individual initial weights varying ± 15 % around 1 800 g initial_weights <- round(runif(n_fish, min = 1800 * 0.85, max = 1800 * 1.15))  # Each fish has its own feeding level drawn from a population distribution # centred on the reference p estimated above (sigma_p = 0.06) true_p <- pmax(0.3, pmin(1.5, rnorm(n_fish, mean = ref_p, sd = 0.06)))  # Simulate final weights: run individual direct simulations + measurement noise final_weights <- vapply(seq_len(n_fish), function(i) {   bio_i <- bio   bio_i$simulation_settings$initial_weight <- initial_weights[i]   res_i <- run_fb4(bio_i, strategy = "direct", p_value = true_p[i])   round(res_i$summary$final_weight * rnorm(1, mean = 1, sd = 0.02), 1) }, numeric(1))  mr_data <- data.frame(   individual_id  = sprintf("fish_%02d", seq_len(n_fish)),   initial_weight = initial_weights,   final_weight   = final_weights )  head(mr_data, 6)

title: Statistical estimation strategies
text: data generated theoretical population mean p = 0.753 (binary-search estimate 2 500 g target) SD = 0.06. 20 fish drawn, realised sample mean 0.746 — value mu_p recover. sigma_p reflects true individual variation (SD = 0.06) 2 % measurement noise added final weights.
code:
res_hier <- run_fb4(   x                = bio,   strategy         = "hierarchical",   backend          = "tmb",   fit_to           = "Weight",   observed_weights = mr_data )  cat(sprintf("Population mean p  (mu_p)   : %.4f
", res_hier$summary$mu_p_estimate)) cat(sprintf("Population SD  p  (sigma_p) : %.4f
", res_hier$summary$sigma_p_estimate)) cat(sprintf("Number of individuals       : %d
",   res_hier$summary$n_individuals)) cat(sprintf("Converged                   : %s
",   res_hier$summary$converged))

title: Statistical estimation strategies
text: mu_p sigma_p biologically meaningful, study design must satisfy conditions. fish data set must belong species life stage must experienced environmental conditions (temperature, diet). mix life stages fish different habitats, mu_p becomes statistical average clear ecological interpretation. model also assumes fixed monitoring period: every individual tracked n_days. Variable recapture intervals currently supported. statistical side, individual p values modelled log scale, implied population distribution approximately log-normal. guarantees p > 0 works well individual variation moderate. model accepts covariate matrix (covariates_matrix) want explain part variation p body size, sex, tagging location; without covariates, unexplained variation accumulates sigma_p. Finally, hierarchical model requires backend = "tmb" — automatic differentiation via TMB needed mixed-effects likelihood. pure-R backend support strategy.

title: Statistical estimation strategies
text: Binary search, MLE, bootstrap answer question — p produces observed growth representative fish? — can therefore compared numerically. three use bio object (initial_weight = 1800 g, target observed weights ≈ 3 000 g). Strategies 1–3 fitted data (Chinook salmon, 100-day simulation, target weight ≈ 3 000 g).

title: Statistical estimation strategies
text: hierarchical model answers different question: p representative individual, distribution p across population tagged fish. output (mu_p, sigma_p) numerically comparable single estimates input data differ (fish initial final weight) parameter estimated population mean, individual feeding level. Hierarchical model results simulated mark-recapture cohort (n = 20 fish, true mu_p = 0.753 ).

Bioenergetics modeling 21st century: reviewing new insights revisiting old constraints. Transactions American Fisheries Society 137(1):298–313.

title: Temperature Sensitivity and Climate Change Scenarios
text: Temperature primary environmental driver fish bioenergetics. metabolic rates scale non-linearly temperature, even modest warming can disproportionate effects growth consumption. vignette uses fb4package : Quantify final weight responds temperature shifts (sensitivity analysis) Project growth outcomes +1 °C, +2 °C, +4 °C warming scenarios Identify thermal performance windows using sensitivity plot Compare sensitivity across two ecologically contrasting species

title: Temperature Sensitivity and Climate Change Scenarios
text: use 120-day summer conditions baseline.
code: # Species parameters (Stewart & Ibarra 1991) sp_params_chinook <- list(   consumption = list(     CEQ = 2, CA = 0.303, CB = -0.275,     CQ = 3.0, CTO = 15.0, CTM = 20.0,     CTL = 24.0, CK1 = 0.1, CK4 = 0.13   ),   respiration = list(     REQ = 1, RA = 0.00264, RB = -0.217,     RQ = 0.06818, RTO = 0.0234, RTM = 0.0,     RTL = 25.0, RK1 = 1.0, RK4 = 0.13, RK5 = 0.0   ),   activity  = list(ACT = 1.0, BACT = 0.0),   sda       = list(SDA = 0.172),   egestion  = list(EGEQ = 1, FA = 0.212, FB = -0.222, FG = 0.631),   excretion = list(EXEQ = 1, UA = 0.0314, UB = 0.58, UG = -0.299),   predator  = list(     PREDEDEQ = 2,     Alpha1 = 4500, Beta1 = 6.0,     Alpha2 = 5500, Beta2 = 2.0,     Cutoff = 250   ) )  days <- 1:120  # Baseline temperature: cool Pacific NW summer temp_base <- data.frame(   Day         = days,   Temperature = round(7 + 6 * sin(pi * (days - 20) / 120), 2) )  # Diet diet_props  <- data.frame(Day = days, Alewife = 0.65, Shrimp = 0.35) prey_energy <- data.frame(Day = days, Alewife = 4900, Shrimp = 3200)  # Build baseline object make_bio <- function(temp_df, sp_params, initial_weight = 5) {   bio <- Bioenergetic(     species_params     = sp_params,     species_info       = list(scientific_name = "Oncorhynchus tshawytscha",                                common_name = "Chinook salmon",                                life_stage  = "juvenile"),     environmental_data = list(temperature = temp_df),     diet_data          = list(       proportions = diet_props,       prey_names  = c("Alewife", "Shrimp"),       energies    = prey_energy     ),     simulation_settings = list(initial_weight = initial_weight, duration = 120)   )   bio }  bio_base <- make_bio(temp_base, sp_params_chinook)  cat(sprintf("Baseline temperature range : %.1f – %.1f °C\n",             min(temp_base$Temperature), max(temp_base$Temperature))) #> Baseline temperature range : 4.1 – 13.0 °C

what: 2. Manual sensitivity analysis across temperature offsets
title: Temperature Sensitivity and Climate Change Scenarios
text: run binary search temperature offset record p required achieve 40 g 120 days (target growth endpoint). p-value required achieve 40 g across temperature offsets Estimated p-value function temperature offset. Interpretation: warmer temperatures, Chinook must feed higher proportion maximum ration achieve growth target, respiration costs increase faster maximum consumption near thermal optimum. ~+3 °C, required p approaches exceeds 1, indicating growth 40 g may become impossible temperatures.
code: offsets   <- c(-3, -2, -1, 0, 1, 2, 3, 4) target_wt <- 40  results_sens <- lapply(offsets, function(delta) {   temp_shifted <- temp_base   temp_shifted$Temperature <- pmax(1, temp_base$Temperature + delta)    bio_s <- make_bio(temp_shifted, sp_params_chinook)   res   <- tryCatch(     run_fb4(bio_s,             fit_to    = "Weight",             fit_value = target_wt,             strategy  = "binary_search",             verbose   = FALSE),     error = function(e) NULL   )    if (is.null(res)) {     return(data.frame(offset = delta, p_value = NA, final_weight = NA))   }   data.frame(     offset       = delta,     p_value      = round(res$summary$p_value,       4),     final_weight = round(res$summary$final_weight,   1)   ) })  sens_df <- do.call(rbind, results_sens) plot(sens_df$offset, sens_df$p_value,      type = "b", pch = 19, lwd = 2, col = "steelblue",      xlab = "Temperature offset (°C)",      ylab = "Estimated p-value",      main = "Feeding level required to achieve target weight") abline(v = 0, lty = 2, col = "gray50") grid()

title: Temperature Sensitivity and Climate Change Scenarios
text: plot(bio, type = "sensitivity") runs analysis internally produces formatted figure directly. Built-temperature sensitivity plot. point shows p required achieve target weight given temperature offset.
code: plot(bio_base,      type         = "sensitivity",      temp_offsets = c(-3, -2, -1, 0, 1, 2, 3, 4),      fit_to       = "Weight",      fit_value    = 40,      colors       = "blue")

title: Temperature Sensitivity and Climate Change Scenarios
text: Rather fixing target weight, fix p = 0.65 (estimated field data) ask: final weight change warming scenarios? Projected outcomes p = 0.65 four temperature scenarios Projected final weight climate change scenarios (p fixed 0.65).
code: p_fixed   <- 0.65 scenarios <- c("Baseline" = 0, "+1 °C" = 1, "+2 °C" = 2, "+4 °C" = 4)  scenario_results <- lapply(names(scenarios), function(sc) {   delta <- scenarios[[sc]]   temp_s <- temp_base   temp_s$Temperature <- pmax(1, temp_base$Temperature + delta)    bio_s <- make_bio(temp_s, sp_params_chinook)   res   <- run_fb4(bio_s,                    fit_to    = "p_value",                    fit_value = p_fixed,                    strategy  = "direct_p_value",                    verbose   = FALSE)    if (is.null(res)) return(NULL)    data.frame(     Scenario    = sc,     Delta_T     = delta,     Final_wt_g  = round(res$summary$final_weight,       1),     Consumption = round(res$summary$total_consumption_g, 1)   ) })  scenario_df <- do.call(rbind, scenario_results) bar_cols <- c("steelblue", "gold", "darkorange", "firebrick") bp <- barplot(scenario_df$Final_wt_g,               names.arg = scenario_df$Scenario,               col       = bar_cols,               ylab      = "Final weight (g)",               main      = paste("Growth at p =", p_fixed,                                 "under warming scenarios"),               ylim      = c(0, max(scenario_df$Final_wt_g) * 1.25),               border    = NA)  text(bp, scenario_df$Final_wt_g + 0.5,      labels = paste0(scenario_df$Final_wt_g, " g"),      cex = 0.85, font = 2) grid(nx = NA, ny = NULL, col = "gray85")

title: Temperature Sensitivity and Climate Change Scenarios
text: metabolism–consumption balance defines thermal performance window: temperature range fish can maintain positive growth (p < 1). can derive finding temperature p = 1 target weight. Thermal performance window: p required achieve 40 g 120 days constant temperatures. shaded region shows feasible window (p ≤ 1).
code: # Sweep a fine temperature grid and record required p temp_grid   <- seq(2, 24, by = 1) p_at_target <- sapply(temp_grid, function(t_const) {   temp_const <- data.frame(Day = days, Temperature = t_const)   bio_t <- make_bio(temp_const, sp_params_chinook)   tryCatch({     res <- run_fb4(bio_t,                    fit_to    = "Weight",                    fit_value = target_wt,                    strategy  = "binary_search",                    verbose   = FALSE)     res$summary$p_value   }, error = function(e) NA_real_) })  perf_df  <- data.frame(Temperature = temp_grid, p_required = p_at_target) feasible <- perf_df[!is.na(perf_df$p_required) & perf_df$p_required <= 1, ] #> Feasible temperature range for 40 g in 120 days : 2 – 19 °C plot(perf_df$Temperature, perf_df$p_required,      type = "l", lwd = 2, col = "steelblue",      xlab = "Constant water temperature (°C)",      ylab = "p required to achieve 40 g",      main = "Thermal performance window — juvenile Chinook",      ylim = c(0, max(perf_df$p_required, na.rm = TRUE) * 1.1))  # Shade feasible zone polygon(c(min(feasible$Temperature), feasible$Temperature,           max(feasible$Temperature)),         c(0, feasible$p_required, 0),         col = adjustcolor("steelblue", alpha.f = 0.2),         border = NA)  abline(h = 1, lty = 2, col = "firebrick", lwd = 1.5) text(max(perf_df$Temperature) - 2, 1.02, "p = 1 (maximum ration)", col = "firebrick", cex = 0.8) grid()

title: Temperature Sensitivity and Climate Change Scenarios
text: analyses direct relevance fish management climate change: Habitat quality: thermal performance window defines suitable temperature ranges stocking conservation planning. Consumption modelling: Holding p fixed warming temperatures leads lower growth warm temperatures, respiration increases faster consumption. Bioenergetics-based monitoring: Changes condition factor (K) monitoring data can translated shifts p using framework, providing mechanistic link climate variability fish condition.

Ready for simulation. comp_params <- process_composition_params(list()) #> Warning: Using default water_fraction = 0.75 df <- analyze_composition_changes(result, comp_params) # }

title: Analyze energy budget from FB4 results — analyze_energy_budget
text: result FB4 result object individual_id Individual ID hierarchical models (NULL population/single individual) confidence_level Confidence level intervals (default 0.95)

title: Analyze energy budget from FB4 results — analyze_energy_budget
text: named list four elements: energy_components list returned       get_energy_budget_uncertainty, containing six component       sub-lists estimate, se, ci_lower,       ci_upper. proportions Named list proportional allocations       (prop_respiration, prop_egestion, prop_excretion,       prop_sda, prop_net), sub-list estimate,       se, ci_lower, ci_upper. NULL       consumption energy zero unavailable. summary_metrics Named list gross_growth_efficiency,       metabolic_scope, assimilation_efficiency sub-lists       (estimate + se). NULL proportions       unavailable. balance_check Named list consumption_energy,       total_allocated, balance_error,       relative_error (numeric) verify mass-balance closure. Plus context scalars method, has_uncertainty,   individual_id.

title: Analyze energy budget from FB4 results — analyze_energy_budget
code: # \donttest{ data(fish4_parameters) sp   <- fish4_parameters[["Oncorhynchus tshawytscha"]]$life_stages$adult info <- fish4_parameters[["Oncorhynchus tshawytscha"]]$species_info bio  <- Bioenergetic(   species_params     = sp,   species_info       = info,   environmental_data = list(     temperature = data.frame(Day = 1:30, Temperature = rep(12, 30))   ),   diet_data = list(     proportions = data.frame(Day = 1:30, Prey1 = 1.0),     energies    = data.frame(Day = 1:30, Prey1 = 5000),     prey_names  = "Prey1"   ),   simulation_settings = list(initial_weight = 100, duration = 30) ) #> Bioenergetic object created for: Oncorhynchus tshawytscha bio$species_params$predator$ED_ini <- 5000 bio$species_params$predator$ED_end <- 5500 result <- run_fb4(bio, strategy = "direct", p_value = 0.5, verbose = FALSE) #> Validation warnings: #> Missing optional parameters (will be calculated): CG1, CG2 #> Object is ready for simulation #> Validation warnings: #> Missing optional parameters (will be calculated): CG1, CG2 #> Object is ready for simulation #> Processing species parameters... #> Processing temporal data... #> No indigestible fraction data provided, using default 0% for all prey (FB4 default) #> Processing simulation settings... #> Simulation data preparation complete. Ready for simulation. budget <- analyze_energy_budget(result) # }

text: named list minimum method (character),   has_uncertainty (logical), individual_id. Additional   elements present relevant data available: total_consumption list returned       get_consumption_uncertainty (estimate, se,       ci_lower, ci_upper, plus context scalars). daily_consumption Sub-list (estimate, se,       ci_lower, ci_upper) daily consumption rate       (g/day). specific_consumption Sub-list (four slots)       specific consumption rate (g consumption / g fish / day). p_value Structure depends method: hierarchical contains       population_mean, population_se, population_sd,       n_individuals; single-individual methods contains       estimate, se, ci_lower, ci_upper. feeding_efficiency Sub-list (estimate, se,       ci_lower, ci_upper) ratio total growth       total consumption (dimensionless).

Ready for simulation. feeding <- analyze_feeding_performance(result) # }

what: Analyze growth rate sensitivity to temperature and feeding levels — analyze_growth_temperature_sensitivity
title: Analyze growth rate sensitivity to temperature and feeding levels — analyze_growth_temperature_sensitivity
text: Analyzes growth rates respond different temperature feeding level combinations. Uses p-values (proportion maximum consumption capacity) simulate feeding scenarios survival levels (p ~0.2) maximum capacity (p = 1.0). Parallelized efficiency.

title: Analyze growth rate sensitivity to temperature and feeding levels — analyze_growth_temperature_sensitivity
code: analyze_growth_temperature_sensitivity(   bio_obj,   temperatures = seq(8, 18, by = 2),   p_values = seq(0.3, 1, by = 0.1),   simulation_days = 365,   oxycal = 13560,   parallel = FALSE,   n_cores = NULL,   verbose = TRUE )

title: Analyze growth rate sensitivity to temperature and feeding levels — analyze_growth_temperature_sensitivity
text: bio_obj Bioenergetic object containing species parameters environmental data temperatures Vector temperatures test °C (default: 8-18°C 2°C steps) p_values Vector p-values representing feeding levels proportion Cmax (default: 0.3-1.0) simulation_days Number days simulate (default: 365) oxycal Oxycalorific coefficient J/g O2 (default: 13560) parallel Use parallel processing (default: FALSE) n_cores Number cores parallel processing (default: detectCores() - 1) verbose Show progress information (default: TRUE)

title: Analyze growth rate sensitivity to temperature and feeding levels — analyze_growth_temperature_sensitivity
text: data frame one row per temperature-p_value combination columns: temperature Temperature tested (°C). p_value Feeding level (proportion Cmax) tested. growth_rate Specific growth rate (percent per day). final_weight Predicted final weight (g). total_consumption Total consumption simulation period (g).

title: Analyze growth rate sensitivity to temperature and feeding levels — analyze_growth_temperature_sensitivity
code: # \donttest{ data(fish4_parameters) sp   <- fish4_parameters[["Oncorhynchus tshawytscha"]]$life_stages$adult info <- fish4_parameters[["Oncorhynchus tshawytscha"]]$species_info bio  <- Bioenergetic(   species_params     = sp,   species_info       = info,   environmental_data = list(     temperature = data.frame(Day = 1:30, Temperature = rep(12, 30))   ),   diet_data = list(     proportions = data.frame(Day = 1:30, Prey1 = 1.0),     energies    = data.frame(Day = 1:30, Prey1 = 5000),     prey_names  = "Prey1"   ),   simulation_settings = list(initial_weight = 100, duration = 30) ) #> Bioenergetic object created for: Oncorhynchus tshawytscha bio$species_params$predator$ED_ini <- 5000 bio$species_params$predator$ED_end <- 5500 results <- analyze_growth_temperature_sensitivity(   bio,   temperatures     = c(2, 14),   p_values         = c(0.4, 0.7),   simulation_days  = 30,   verbose          = FALSE ) #> Processing species parameters... #> Processing temporal data... #> No indigestible fraction data provided, using default 0% for all prey (FB4 default) #> Processing simulation settings... #> Simulation data preparation complete. Ready for simulation. # }

title: Constructor for Bioenergetic Objects — Bioenergetic
text: Creates Bioenergetic class object encapsulates components fish bioenergetic model streamlined simulation management.

title: Constructor for Bioenergetic Objects — Bioenergetic
code: Bioenergetic(   species_params,   species_info = NULL,   environmental_data = NULL,   diet_data = NULL,   reproduction_data = NULL,   nutrient_data = NULL,   contaminant_data = NULL,   model_options = list(),   simulation_settings = list() )

title: Constructor for Bioenergetic Objects — Bioenergetic
text: species_params List species parameters organized categories species_info List species identification information environmental_data List environmental data (temperature, etc.) diet_data List diet prey energy data reproduction_data List reproduction parameters (optional) nutrient_data Optional list nitrogen phosphorus data nutrient sub-model. Expected fields: N_conc, P_conc, N_assim, P_assim (assimilation efficiencies). NULL (default) nutrient sub-model disabled. contaminant_data Optional list contaminant data contaminant sub-model. Expected fields depend CONTEQ equation selected (pure accumulation, T/W-dependent elimination, Arnot Gobas 2004). NULL (default) contaminant sub-model disabled. model_options List model configuration options simulation_settings List simulation configuration

title: Constructor for Bioenergetic Objects — Bioenergetic
text: object class "Bioenergetic": named list eight   elements: species_info, species_params,   environmental_data, diet_data, reproduction_data,   model_options, simulation_settings, fitted   (logical, FALSE simulation run). results   element appended set_environment,   set_diet, run_fb4 reset   populate object.

title: Constructor for Bioenergetic Objects — Bioenergetic
text: Bioenergetic object serves comprehensive container bioenergetic model components. Required Components: species_params Parameter sets consumption, respiration, etc. species_info Species identification scientific_name common_name Optional Components: environmental_data Temperature environmental variables diet_data Diet composition prey energy densities model_options Sub-model toggles advanced settings simulation_settings Initial conditions duration

title: Constructor for Bioenergetic Objects — Bioenergetic
code: # \donttest{ # Create species parameters params <- list(   consumption = list(CEQ = 2, CA = 0.303, CB = -0.275, CQ = 3, CTO = 15, CTM = 25),   respiration = list(REQ = 1, RA = 0.0548, RB = -0.299, RQ = 2, RTO = 5, RTM = 25) )  # Create species info species_info <- list(   scientific_name = "Salmo salar",   common_name = "Atlantic salmon",   life_stage = "juvenile" )  # Create bioenergetic object bio_obj <- Bioenergetic(   species_params = params,   species_info = species_info,   simulation_settings = list(initial_weight = 10, duration = 365) ) #> Bioenergetic object created for: Salmo salar # }

what: Body Composition Functions for FB4 Model — body-composition
title: Body Composition Functions for FB4 Model — body-composition
text: Functions estimating updating fish body composition (water, protein, ash, fat) energy density total wet weight. Composition estimated via allometric regressions Breck (2014): $$\log_{10}(\text{Component}) = \alpha + \beta \cdot \log_{10}(H_2O)$$ Fat obtained subtraction bounded biologically plausible limits. Energy density computed weighted sum fat protein energy contents (J/g).

title: Body Composition Functions for FB4 Model — body-composition
text: return value; page documents body composition functions module. See individual function documentation return values.

title: Body Composition Functions for FB4 Model — body-composition

code: calculate_body_composition(weight, processed_composition_params)

title: Calculate complete body composition (Mid-level - Main function) — calculate_body_composition
text: weight Total wet weight (g) processed_composition_params List processed composition parameters

title: Calculate complete body composition (Mid-level - Main function) — calculate_body_composition
text: named list 13 elements describing body composition: total_weight Numeric. Total wet weight (g), equal weight. water_g Numeric. Water content (g). protein_g Numeric. Protein content (g), estimated water via       Breck (2014) regression. ash_g Numeric. Ash content (g), estimated water via Breck       (2014) regression. fat_g Numeric. Fat content (g), calculated subtraction       bounded [0, max_fat_fraction * weight]. water_fraction Numeric. Water fraction total weight. protein_fraction Numeric. Protein fraction total weight. ash_fraction Numeric. Ash fraction total weight. fat_fraction Numeric. Fat fraction total weight. energy_density Numeric. Energy density (J/g wet weight). total_energy Numeric. Total body energy (J). total_fraction Numeric. Sum four fractions;       close 1. balanced Logical. TRUE total_fraction within       0.05 1. Returns create_empty_composition() weight <= 0.

title: Calculate complete body composition (Mid-level - Main function) — calculate_body_composition
code: params <- list(water_fraction = 0.72, fat_energy = 36450,                protein_energy = 17990, max_fat_fraction = 0.30) calculate_body_composition(weight = 100,                            processed_composition_params = params) #> $total_weight #> [1] 100 #>  #> $water_g #> [1] 72 #>  #> $protein_g #> [1] 15.48219 #>  #> $ash_g #> [1] 1.787121 #>  #> $fat_g #> [1] 10.73069 #>  #> $water_fraction #> [1] 0.72 #>  #> $protein_fraction #> [1] 0.1548219 #>  #> $ash_fraction #> [1] 0.01787121 #>  #> $fat_fraction #> [1] 0.1073069 #>  #> $energy_density #> [1] 6696.582 #>  #> $total_energy #> [1] 669658.2 #>  #> $total_fraction #> [1] 1 #>  #> $balanced #> [1] TRUE #>

Returns 0   temperature-dependence factor zero (e.g. temperature \(\ge\) CTM   equation 2). value depends method: "rate" (default)   scales p_value (\(C_{\max} \cdot p \cdot F(T)\)); "maximum"   "specific" return unscaled value (\(C_{\max} \cdot F(T)\)).

title: Calculate contaminant accumulation (Mid-level - Main function) — calculate_contaminant_accumulation
text: respiration_o2 Respiration g O2/g/day consumption Vector consumption prey type (g/day) weight Fish weight (g) temperature Water temperature (deg C) current_concentration Current concentration predator (ug/g) processed_contaminant_params List processed contaminant parameters

title: Calculate contaminant accumulation (Mid-level - Main function) — calculate_contaminant_accumulation
text: named list least six elements (numeric scalars unless   noted): clearance Daily elimination contaminant (ug/day). uptake Total daily uptake food (ug/day); CONTEQ 3       sum water food uptake. new_burden Body burden end day (ug); floored 0. new_concentration Whole-body concentration (ug/g wet weight);       floored 0. weight Fish weight (g), supplied. model_used Integer. CONTEQ equation used (1, 2, 3). CONTEQ 3 (Arnot & Gobas 2004) appends two additional elements:   uptake_water (ug/day water) uptake_food   (ug/day food).

title: Calculate contaminant accumulation (Mid-level - Main function) — calculate_contaminant_accumulation
text: Contaminant modelling **experimental feature** active development. function can called directly compute daily bioaccumulation single time step, **yet integrated** main `run_fb4()` simulation loop. Full integration (automatic contaminant tracking across simulation days, inclusion `fb4_result` objects, TMB backend support) planned future release. API may change.

title: Calculate contaminant accumulation (Mid-level - Main function) — calculate_contaminant_accumulation
code: # CONTEQ 1: food uptake only, no elimination params <- list(   CONTEQ = 1,   prey_concentrations = c(0.05, 0.08),   transfer_efficiency = c(0.8, 0.8) ) calculate_contaminant_accumulation(   respiration_o2 = 0.02, consumption = c(2.0, 1.0),   weight = 100, temperature = 15,   current_concentration = 0.1,   processed_contaminant_params = params ) #> $clearance #> [1] 0 #>  #> $uptake #> [1] 0.144 #>  #> $new_burden #> [1] 10.144 #>  #> $new_concentration #> [1] 0.10144 #>  #> $weight #> [1] 100 #>  #> $model_used #> [1] 1 #>

text: named list five elements: mortality Named list seven numeric scalars (daily       rates, 0–1): survival_rate, combined_mortality,       natural_mortality, fishing_mortality,       predation_mortality, weight_effect (increment       mortality due low body weight), temperature_effect       (increment due thermal stress). reproduction NULL spawn pattern defined       processed_mortality_params; otherwise named list       weight_loss (g), energy_loss (J),       spawn_fraction (0–1), remaining_weight (g). day_of_year Integer. title: Calculate daily mortality and reproduction (Mid-level - Main function) — calculate_mortality_reproduction
text: Mortality rate modelling **experimental feature** active development. function can called directly compute daily mortality probability single time step, **mortality rates yet integrated** main `run_fb4()` simulation loop. Full integration (automatic daily mortality application, population survival tracking, result reporting) planned future release. API may change. Note: **spawning energy loss** (reproductive cost) ** already integrated `run_fb4()` applies automatically `reproduction_data` supplied `Bioenergetic` object.

title: Calculate daily mortality and reproduction (Mid-level - Main function) — calculate_mortality_reproduction
code: params <- list(   base_mortality      = 0.001,   natural_mortality   = 0.001,   fishing_mortality   = 0.0005,   predation_mortality = 0.0002,   weight_threshold    = 10,   starvation_factor   = 2,   optimal_temp        = 18,   thermal_tolerance   = 8,   stress_factor       = 1.5,   spawn_pattern       = NULL ) calculate_mortality_reproduction(current_weight = 100, temperature = 15,                                  day_of_year = 180,                                  processed_mortality_params = params) #> $mortality #> $mortality$survival_rate #> [1] 0.9983008 #>  #> $mortality$combined_mortality #> [1] 0.0016992 #>  #> $mortality$natural_mortality #> [1] 0.001 #>  #> $mortality$fishing_mortality #> [1] 5e-04 #>  #> $mortality$predation_mortality #> [1] 2e-04 #>  #> $mortality$weight_effect #> [1] 0 #>  #> $mortality$temperature_effect #> [1] 0 #>  #>  #> $reproduction #> NULL #>  #> $day_of_year #> [1] 180 #>  #> $current_weight #> [1] 100 #>  #> $temperature #> [1] 15 #>

title: Calculate N:P ratios for all processes — calculate_np_ratios
text: named list three elements: ratios Named numeric vector length 4 giving N:P ratio       process (consumed, growth, excretion,       egestion). Values may Inf phosphorus zero       NaN nutrients zero. ratio_type Character. ratio type supplied ("mass"       "molar"). redfield_ratio Numeric. Reference Redfield ratio: 7.2 mass       ratios 16 molar ratios.

title: Calculate N:P ratios for all processes — calculate_np_ratios
code: nitrogen   <- list(consumed = 10, growth = 3, excretion = 5, egestion = 2) phosphorus <- list(consumed = 1.5, growth = 0.5, excretion = 0.6, egestion = 0.4) np_ratios  <- calculate_np_ratios(nitrogen, phosphorus) np_ratios$ratios #>  consumed    growth excretion  egestion  #>  6.666667  6.000000  8.333333  5.000000

title: Calculate nutrient balance (Mid-level - Main function) — calculate_nutrient_balance
code: params <- list(   prey_n_concentrations     = c(0.025, 0.030),   prey_p_concentrations     = c(0.004, 0.005),   predator_n_concentration  = 0.030,   predator_p_concentration  = 0.004,   n_assimilation_efficiency = c(0.80, 0.80),   p_assimilation_efficiency = c(0.60, 0.60) ) calculate_nutrient_balance(consumption = c(2.0, 1.0),                            weight_gain = 0.5,                            processed_nutrient_params = params) #> $nitrogen #> $nitrogen$consumed #> [1] 0.08 #>  #> $nitrogen$growth #> [1] 0.015 #>  #> $nitrogen$excretion #> [1] 0.049 #>  #> $nitrogen$egestion #> [1] 0.016 #>  #> $nitrogen$assimilated #> [1] 0.064 #>  #> $nitrogen$assimilation_efficiency #> [1] 0.8 #>  #>  #> $phosphorus #> $phosphorus$consumed #> [1] 0.013 #>  #> $phosphorus$growth #> [1] 0.002 #>  #> $phosphorus$excretion #> [1] 0.0058 #>  #> $phosphorus$egestion #> [1] 0.0052 #>  #> $phosphorus$assimilated #> [1] 0.0078 #>  #> $phosphorus$assimilation_efficiency #> [1] 0.6 #>  #>  #> $weight_gain #> [1] 0.5 #>

title: Calculate nutrient retention efficiencies — calculate_nutrient_efficiencies
text: named list four elements: nitrogen Named list four numeric scalars:       assimilation_efficiency (fraction consumed assimilated),       retention_efficiency (fraction consumed retained growth),       excretion_rate (fraction consumed lost via excretion),       growth_efficiency (fraction assimilated retained growth). phosphorus structure nitrogen       phosphorus. relative_n_retention Numeric. Ratio nitrogen phosphorus       retention efficiency; NA phosphorus retention zero. relative_n_excretion Numeric. Ratio nitrogen phosphorus       excretion rate; NA phosphorus excretion rate zero.

title: Calculate stoichiometric balance — calculate_stoichiometric_balance
text: named list ten elements: nutrient_limitation Character. Overall assessment:       "N-limited", "P-limited", "Undetermined". limiting_nutrient Character. identified limiting nutrient       ("nitrogen", "phosphorus", "unknown"). excess_nutrient Character. nutrient relative excess. excess_factor Numeric. code: # \donttest{ data(fish4_parameters) sp   <- fish4_parameters[["Oncorhynchus tshawytscha"]]$life_stages$adult info <- fish4_parameters[["Oncorhynchus tshawytscha"]]$species_info bio  <- Bioenergetic(   species_params     = sp,   species_info       = info,   environmental_data = list(     temperature = data.frame(Day = 1:30, Temperature = rep(12, 30))   ),   diet_data = list(     proportions = data.frame(Day = 1:30, Prey1 = 1.0),     energies    = data.frame(Day = 1:30, Prey1 = 5000),     prey_names  = "Prey1"   ),   simulation_settings = list(initial_weight = 100, duration = 30) ) #> Bioenergetic object created for: Oncorhynchus tshawytscha bio$species_params$predator$ED_ini <- 5000 bio$species_params$predator$ED_end <- 5500 mr_data <- data.frame(   individual_id  = paste0("fish_", 1:5),   initial_weight = c(10, 12, 11, 13, 9),   final_weight   = c(80, 95, 85, 100, 70) ) result_hier <- run_fb4(bio, strategy = "hierarchical", backend = "tmb",                        fit_to = "Weight", observed_weights = mr_data,                        verbose = FALSE) #> Validation warnings: #> Missing optional parameters (will be calculated): CG1, CG2 #> Object is ready for simulation #> Processing species parameters... #> Processing temporal data... #> No indigestible fraction data provided, using default 0% for all prey (FB4 default) #> Processing simulation settings... #> Validation warnings: #> Missing optional parameters (will be calculated): CG1, CG2 #> Object is ready for simulation #> Validation warnings: #> Missing optional parameters (will be calculated): CG1, CG2 #> Object is ready for simulation #> Processing species parameters... #> Processing temporal data... #> No indigestible fraction data provided, using default 0% for all prey (FB4 default) #> Processing simulation settings... #> Simulation data preparation complete. Ready for simulation. comparison <- compare_individuals(result_hier) # }

what: Consumption Functions for FB4 Model — consumption-functions
title: Consumption Functions for FB4 Model — consumption-functions
text: Functions implementing four consumption temperature-dependence equations (CEQ 1–4) allometric maximum-consumption function used FB4. Consumption modelled : $$C = C_{\max} \cdot p \cdot F(T), \quad C_{\max} = CA \cdot W^{CB}$$ \(p\) proportion maximum consumption (P-value), \(F(T)\) temperature-dependence function, \(W\) body mass (g), \(CA\), \(CB\) species-specific intercept slope coefficients. CEQ 1 — simple Q10 exponential: \(F(T) = e^{CQ \cdot T}\) CEQ 2 — Kitchell et al. (1977): \(F(T) = V^{CX} \cdot e^{CX(1-V)}\), \(V = (CTM - T)/(CTM - CTO)\) CEQ 3 — Thornton Lessem (1978): two-part sigmoid using \(CQ\), \(CTO\), \(CTL\), \(CTM\), \(CK1\), \(CK4\) CEQ 4 — polynomial: \(F(T) = e^{CQ \cdot T + CK1 \cdot T^2 + CK4 \cdot T^3}\)

title: Consumption Functions for FB4 Model — consumption-functions
text: return value; page documents consumption functions module. See individual function documentation return values.

title: Consumption Functions for FB4 Model — consumption-functions
text: Kitchell, J.F., Stewart, D.J. Weininger, D. (1977). Applications bioenergetics model yellow perch walleye. Journal Fisheries Research Board Canada, 34(10), 1922–1935. doi:10.1139/f77-258 Thornton, K.W. Lessem, .S. (1978). temperature algorithm modifying biological rates. Transactions American Fisheries Society, 107(2), 284–287. Hartman, K.J. Hayward, R.S. (2007). Bioenergetics. C.S. Guy M.L. Brown (eds.), Analysis Interpretation Freshwater Fisheries Data. American Fisheries Society, Bethesda, MD. Deslauriers, D., Chipps, S.R., Breck, J.E., Rice, J.. Madenjian, C.P. (2017). Fish Bioenergetics 4.0: R-based modeling application. Fisheries, 42(11), 586–596. doi:10.1080/03632415.2017.1377558

what: Contaminant Accumulation Functions for FB4 Model — contaminant-accumulation
title: Contaminant Accumulation Functions for FB4 Model — contaminant-accumulation
text: Experimental functions modelling daily contaminant (e.g. methylmercury, PCBs) dynamics fish using three bioaccumulation models (CONTEQ 1–3). CONTEQ 1 — food uptake , elimination: \(\text{Burden}_{t+1} = \text{Burden}_t + \sum_i C_i \cdot [\text{prey}]_i \cdot \text{AE}_i\) CONTEQ 2 — food uptake temperature- weight-dependent elimination (Trudel Rasmussen 1997): \(K_x = \exp(0.066 T - 0.2 \ln W - 6.56)\) CONTEQ 3 — Arnot Gobas (2004): uptake water (via gill transfer) food, elimination proportional respiration.

Rasmussen, J.B. (1997). Modeling elimination mercury fish. Environmental Science Technology, 31(6), 1716–1722. doi:10.1021/es960609t
