what: Egestion and Excretion Functions for FB4 Model — egestion-excretion
title: Egestion and Excretion Functions for FB4 Model — egestion-excretion
text: Functions implementing four egestion models (EGEQ 1–4) four excretion models (EXEQ 1–4). represent losses consumed energy feces (F) nitrogenous wastes (U) respectively. Egestion models: EGEQ 1 — constant fraction: \(F = FA \cdot C\) EGEQ 2 — Elliott (1976), temperature- feeding-dependent: \(F = FA \cdot T^{FB} \cdot e^{FG \cdot p} \cdot C\) EGEQ 3 — Stewart et al. (1983), includes indigestible fraction: modified form EGEQ 2 accounting indigestible prey material. EGEQ 4 — Elliott (1976), temperature-dependent : \(F = FA \cdot T^{FB} \cdot C\) Excretion models: EXEQ 1 — constant fraction assimilated energy: \(U = UA \cdot (C - F)\) EXEQ 2–3 — Elliott (1976), temperature- feeding-dependent: \(U = UA \cdot T^{UB} \cdot e^{UG \cdot p} \cdot (C - F)\) EXEQ 4 — temperature-dependent .

(1983). energetics model lake trout, Salvelinus namaycush: application Lake Michigan population. Canadian Journal Fisheries Aquatic Sciences, 40(6), 681–698. title: Fish Bioenergetics 4.0 Package — fb4package-package
text: R package implementation Fish Bioenergetics 4.0 (FB4), widely used approach modelling energy allocation fish. model partitions consumed energy three fundamental components: metabolism (respiration + activity + specific dynamic action), waste losses (egestion + excretion), growth (somatic + gonadal). package provides multiple estimation strategies (binary search, direct, bootstrap, maximum likelihood) optional C++ backend via TMB high-performance MLE.

title: Fish Bioenergetics 4.0 Package — fb4package-package
text: bioenergetics energy balance implemented follows Kitchell et al. (1977): $$C = R + + \mathrm{SDA} + F + U + G$$ \(C\) = consumption, \(R\) = standard respiration, \(\) = active metabolism, \(\mathrm{SDA}\) = specific dynamic action, \(F\) = egestion, \(U\) = excretion, \(G\) = growth (somatic + gonadal). Consumption modelled \(C = C_{\max} \cdot p \cdot F(T)\), \(C_{\max} = CA \cdot W^{CB}\) (Hartman Hayward 2007) \(F(T)\) one four temperature-dependence functions (CEQ 1–4; Kitchell et al. 1977; Thornton Lessem 1978). Respiration modelled \(R = RA \cdot W^{RB} \cdot F(T) \cdot \mathrm{ACT}\), \(F(T)\) following Kitchell et al. (1977) simple Q10 exponential (REQ 1–2). Egestion excretion follow Elliott (1976) Stewart et al. (1983) (EGEQ/EXEQ 1–4). Predator energy density can modelled weight-dependent constant (PREDEDEQ 1–2; Hanson et al. 1997). package extends Fish Bioenergetics 4.0 (Deslauriers et al. 2017), R-based re-implementation Fish Bioenergetics 3.0 (Hanson et al. 1997) predecessors (Hewett Johnson 1987, 1992).

title: Fish Bioenergetics 4.0 Official Parameters Database — fish4_parameters
text: list containing bioenergetic parameters fish species following structure: species_name List species containing: species_info Basic taxonomic information (scientific name, common name, family, order) life_stages Named list life stages (e.g., "juvenile", "adult", "larval") containing: consumption Consumption parameters (CA, CB, CQ, CTO, CTM, CTL, CK1, CK4, CEQ) respiration Respiration parameters (RA, RB, RQ, RTO, RTM, RTL, RK1, RK4, RK5, REQ) activity Activity multipliers (ACT, BACT) sda Specific Dynamic Action coefficient (SDA) egestion Egestion parameters (FA, FB, FG, EGEQ) excretion Excretion parameters (UA, UB, UG, EXEQ) predator Predator energy density parameters (Alpha1, Beta1, Alpha2, Beta2, Cutoff, ED_data, PREDEDEQ) source Literature source reference notes Additional notes parameters sources Vector literature sources species

title: Fish Bioenergetics 4.0 Official Parameters Database — fish4_parameters
text: Parameters extracted Parameters_official.csv, official species database bundled Fish Bioenergetics 4.0 ('FB4') Shiny application (<https://github.com/biofish/FishBioenergetics>). Converted R list format via generate_fish4_parameters(). Original parameter values compiled peer-reviewed literature; see source field within species entry individual references.

title: Fish Bioenergetics 4.0 Official Parameters Database — fish4_parameters
text: database contains parameters fish species multiple families including Salmonidae, Percidae, Centrarchidae, Cyprinidae, others. species entry includes one life stages complete partial parameter sets. Parameter Categories: Consumption: Temperature-dependent consumption model parameters Respiration: Metabolic rate activity parameters Egestion: Waste production defecation parameters Excretion: Nitrogenous waste excretion parameters Predator Energy Density: Weight-dependent energy content parameters Temperature Parameters: CTO, RTO: Optimum temperature consumption/respiration CTM, RTM: Maximum temperature consumption/respiration CTL, RTL: Lethal temperature consumption/respiration Usage: database primarily used Bioenergetic constructor create species-specific bioenergetic model objects. Parameters can extracted using utility functions accessed directly species life stage.

text: result FB4 result object individual_id Individual ID hierarchical models (NULL population mean) confidence_level Confidence level intervals (default 0.95)

title: Get consumption results with uncertainty — get_consumption_uncertainty
text: named list eight elements: estimate Numeric. Total consumption estimate (g) simulation       period; NA unavailable. se Numeric. Standard error estimate; NA methods       without uncertainty quantification (e.g. "direct",       "binary_search", "optim"). ci_lower Numeric. Lower bound confidence interval;       NA se unavailable. ci_upper Numeric. Upper bound confidence interval;       NA se unavailable. method Character. Fitting method used (e.g. "direct",       "mle", "hierarchical"). backend Character. Computational backend ("r"       "tmb"). has_uncertainty Logical. TRUE standard errors       confidence intervals populated. individual_id supplied; requested individual index,       NULL population mean.

title: Get consumption results with uncertainty — get_consumption_uncertainty
code: # \donttest{ data(fish4_parameters) sp   <- fish4_parameters[["Oncorhynchus tshawytscha"]]$life_stages$adult info <- fish4_parameters[["Oncorhynchus tshawytscha"]]$species_info bio  <- Bioenergetic(   species_params     = sp,   species_info       = info,   environmental_data = list(     temperature = data.frame(Day = 1:30, Temperature = rep(12, 30))   ),   diet_data = list(     proportions = data.frame(Day = 1:30, Prey1 = 1.0),     energies    = data.frame(Day = 1:30, Prey1 = 5000),     prey_names  = "Prey1"   ),   simulation_settings = list(initial_weight = 100, duration = 30) ) #> Bioenergetic object created for: Oncorhynchus tshawytscha bio$species_params$predator$ED_ini <- 5000 bio$species_params$predator$ED_end <- 5500 result <- run_fb4(bio, strategy = "direct", p_value = 0.5, verbose = FALSE) #> Validation warnings: #> Missing optional parameters (will be calculated): CG1, CG2 #> Object is ready for simulation #> Validation warnings: #> Missing optional parameters (will be calculated): CG1, CG2 #> Object is ready for simulation #> Processing species parameters... #> Processing temporal data... #> No indigestible fraction data provided, using default 0% for all prey (FB4 default) #> Processing simulation settings... #> Simulation data preparation complete. Ready for simulation. consumption <- get_consumption_uncertainty(result) # }

what: Predator Energy Density Functions for FB4 Model — predator-energy-density
title: Predator Energy Density Functions for FB4 Model — predator-energy-density
text: Functions implementing three predator energy density models (PREDEDEQ 1–3) corresponding weight-solving routines used FB4 energy balance. PREDEDEQ 1 — interpolated daily data: energy density supplied vector length \(n_{\text{days}} + 1\) (one value per day boundary). PREDEDEQ 2 — piecewise linear weight: \(ED = \alpha_1 + \beta_1 W\) cutoff, \(ED = \alpha_2 + \beta_2 W\) . PREDEDEQ 3 — power function: \(ED = \alpha_1 \cdot W^{\beta_1}\).

title: Predator Energy Density Functions for FB4 Model — predator-energy-density
text: return value; page documents predator energy density functions module. See individual function documentation return values.

title: Predator Energy Density Functions for FB4 Model — predator-energy-density
text: Hanson, P.C., Johnson, T.B., Schindler, D.E. Kitchell, J.F. (1997). Fish Bioenergetics 3.0. University Wisconsin Sea Grant Institute, Madison, WI. Deslauriers, D., Chipps, S.R., Breck, J.E., Rice, J.. Madenjian, C.P. (2017). Fish Bioenergetics 4.0: R-based modeling application. Fisheries, 42(11), 586–596. doi:10.1080/03632415.2017.1377558

title: Energy density from interpolated data - Equation 1 (Low-level) — predator_energy_eq1

code: predator_energy_eq1(weight, day, energy_data)

title: Energy density from interpolated data - Equation 1 (Low-level) — predator_energy_eq1
text: weight Fish weight (g) - used equation day Simulation day (integer, 1-based) energy_data Numeric vector energy densities (J/g). Must length n_days + 1 — one value per day boundary, start day 1 end last day. 365-day simulation, provide 366 values. Use approx(..., xout = 0:n_days) seq(ED_ini, ED_end, length.= n_days + 1) generate vector. initial final values known, use ED_ini/ED_end predator parameters instead.

what: Bootstrap method for consumption uncertainty propagation — predict_consumption_bootstrap
title: Bootstrap method for consumption uncertainty propagation — predict_consumption_bootstrap
text: Propagates p-value uncertainty consumption predictions using parametric bootstrap. Generates multiple samples p-value distribution runs FB4 simulations sample. Provides full uncertainty distribution without linearity assumptions. Supports parallel processing improved performance.

title: Bootstrap method for consumption uncertainty propagation — predict_consumption_bootstrap
code: predict_consumption_bootstrap(   p_mean,   p_sd,   bio_obj,   n_sims = 1000,   first_day = 1,   last_day = 365,   parallel = FALSE,   n_cores = NULL,   confidence_level = 0.95,   verbose = FALSE )

title: Bootstrap method for consumption uncertainty propagation — predict_consumption_bootstrap
text: p_mean Mean p-value distribution p_sd Standard deviation p-value distribution bio_obj Bioenergetic object simulation settings environmental data n_sims Number bootstrap simulations, default 1000 first_day First simulation day, default 1 last_day Last simulation day, default 365 parallel Use parallel processing, default FALSE n_cores Number cores parallel processing (NULL = auto-detect), default NULL confidence_level Confidence level intervals, default 0.95 verbose Show progress messages, default FALSE

title: Bootstrap method for consumption uncertainty propagation — predict_consumption_bootstrap
text: named list elements: method Character string "bootstrap". consumption_mean Mean total consumption across bootstrap samples (g). consumption_sd Standard deviation consumption across samples (g). consumption_ci Numeric vector length 2 lower upper       quantile-based confidence interval bounds (g). consumption_samples Numeric vector successful consumption       estimates bootstrap samples. n_successful Number bootstrap iterations produced valid       consumption estimates. p_mean supplied p_mean value. p_sd supplied p_sd value.

title: Bootstrap method for consumption uncertainty propagation — predict_consumption_bootstrap
text: bootstrap method: 1. Samples p-values Normal(p_mean, p_sd) 2. Constrains samples valid range [0.01, 5.0] 3. Runs FB4 simulation p-value sample 4. Summarizes consumption distribution Parallel processing can significantly reduce computation time large n_sims. method handles simulation failures gracefully reports success rates.

what: Delta method for consumption uncertainty propagation — predict_consumption_delta
title: Delta method for consumption uncertainty propagation — predict_consumption_delta
text: Propagates p-value uncertainty consumption predictions using delta method. Computes numerical derivatives applies first-order approximation uncertainty propagation. Suitable relationship p consumption approximately linear.

title: Delta method for consumption uncertainty propagation — predict_consumption_delta
code: predict_consumption_delta(   p_est,   p_se,   bio_obj,   delta_size = 0.001,   first_day = 1,   last_day = 365,   verbose = FALSE )

title: Delta method for consumption uncertainty propagation — predict_consumption_delta
text: p_est Estimated p-value (feeding level parameter) p_se Standard error p-value estimate bio_obj Bioenergetic object simulation settings environmental data delta_size Small increment numerical derivative computation, default 0.001 first_day First simulation day, default 1 last_day Last simulation day, default 365 verbose Show progress messages, default FALSE

title: Delta method for consumption uncertainty propagation — predict_consumption_delta
text: named list elements: method Character string "delta". consumption_est Point estimate total consumption (g). consumption_se Standard error consumption estimate (g). consumption_ci Numeric vector length 2 lower upper       confidence interval bounds (g). derivative Numerical derivative consumption respect p. linearity_check Logical; TRUE linearity assumption       appears satisfied. p_est supplied p_est value. p_se supplied p_se value.

title: Delta method for consumption uncertainty propagation — predict_consumption_delta
text: delta method uses first-order Taylor series approximation: Var(f(X)) ~ [f'(mu)]^2 * Var(X) linearity check verifies derivative times delta_size small relative consumption estimate, indicating local linearity.

title: Prepare all simulation data — prepare_simulation_data
text: bio_obj Bioenergetic object (must pre-validated) strategy Strategy use: "binary_search", "optim", "bootstrap", "mle", "hierarchical" fit_to Target type fitting (e.g., "Weight"); optional direct strategy fit_value Target value fit ; optional direct strategy first_day First simulation day last_day Last simulation day validate_inputs Whether perform comprehensive validation, default TRUE oxycal Oxycalorific coefficient (J/g O2), default 13560 output_format Output format: "simulation", "tmb_basic", "tmb_hierarchical" observed_weights Data frame columns: individual_id, initial_weight observed_weight covariates Optional covariate matrix data frame choose column individual_data

title: Prepare all simulation data — prepare_simulation_data

what: Respiration Functions for FB4 Model — respiration-functions
title: Respiration Functions for FB4 Model — respiration-functions
text: Functions implementing two respiration temperature-dependence equations (REQ 1–2), activity correction, conversion oxygen consumption energy units. Respiration modelled : $$R = RA \cdot W^{RB} \cdot F(T) \cdot \mathrm{ACT}$$ \(RA\) \(RB\) species-specific intercept slope coefficients, \(W\) body mass (g), \(F(T)\) temperature function, \(\mathrm{ACT}\) activity multiplier. REQ 1 — simple Q10 exponential activity: \(F(T) = e^{RQ \cdot T}\); velocity-based activity Kitchell et al. (1977). REQ 2 — Kitchell et al. (1977): \(F(T) = V^{RX} \cdot e^{RX(1-V)}\), \(V = (RTM - T)/(RTM - RTO)\). Oxygen consumption converted energy using oxycalorific coefficient (default 13 560 J g\(^{-1}\) O\(_2\); Elliott Davison 1975).

title: Respiration Functions for FB4 Model — respiration-functions
text: return value; page documents respiration functions module. See individual function documentation return values.

title: Respiration Functions for FB4 Model — respiration-functions
text: Kitchell, J.F., Stewart, D.J. Weininger, D. (1977). Applications bioenergetics model yellow perch walleye. Journal Fisheries Research Board Canada, 34(10), 1922–1935. doi:10.1139/f77-258 Elliott, J.M. Davison, W. (1975). Energy equivalents oxygen consumption animal energetics. Oecologia, 19(3), 195–201. doi:10.1007/BF00345305 Hanson, P.C., Johnson, T.B., Schindler, D.E. Kitchell, J.F. (1997). Fish Bioenergetics 3.0. University Wisconsin Sea Grant Institute, Madison, WI. Deslauriers, D., Chipps, S.R., Breck, J.E., Rice, J.. Madenjian, C.P. (2017). Fish Bioenergetics 4.0: R-based modeling application. Fisheries, 42(11), 586–596. doi:10.1080/03632415.2017.1377558

text: Temperature factor

title: Temperature function for respiration - Equation 2 (Low-level) — respiration_temp_eq2
text: function calculates temperature effects respiration using: V = (RTM - temperature) / (RTM - RTO) ft = V^RX × exp(RX × (1 - V)) Special cases: - temperature >= RTM: returns 0.000001 (lethal temperature) - ft < 0: returns 0.000001 (mathematical protection) Note: Negative values can occur certain RX parameters, hence minimum bound.

what: Run FB4 simulation on Bioenergetic object — run_fb4.Bioenergetic
title: Run FB4 simulation on Bioenergetic object — run_fb4.Bioenergetic
text: S3 method automatic backend selection bootstrap estimation. Supports traditional optimization methods, MLE approaches, new bootstrap estimation final weight data. main entry point coordinates FB4 execution strategies.

title: Run FB4 simulation on Bioenergetic object — run_fb4.Bioenergetic
code: # S3 method for class 'Bioenergetic' run_fb4(   x,   fit_to = NULL,   fit_value = NULL,   observed_weights = NULL,   covariates = NULL,   first_day = 1,   last_day = NULL,   backend = "r",   strategy = "binary_search",   oxycal = 13560,   tolerance = 0.001,   max_iterations = 25,   optim_method = "Brent",   lower = 0.01,   upper = 5,   hessian = FALSE,   verbose = FALSE,   confidence_level = 0.95,   estimate_sigma = TRUE,   compute_profile = FALSE,   profile_grid_size = 50,   n_bootstrap = 1000,   parallel = FALSE,   n_cores = NULL,   sample_size = NULL,   compute_percentiles = TRUE,   ... )

title: Run FB4 simulation on Bioenergetic object — run_fb4.Bioenergetic
text: x Bioenergetic object model components fit_to Target type: "Weight", "Consumption", "p_value", "Ration", "Ration_prey" fit_value Target value deterministic approach observed_weights Vector observed final weights MLE bootstrap approaches (optional) covariates Optional covariate matrix data frame first_day First simulation day, default 1 last_day Last simulation day (auto-detected NULL) backend Backend selection: "r" (pure R) "tmb" (C++ via TMB, faster MLE) strategy Fitting strategy: "binary_search" (default), "direct", "optim", "mle" (maximum likelihood), "bootstrap" (bootstrap estimation) oxycal Oxycalorific coefficient (J/g O2), default 13560 tolerance Convergence tolerance iterative fitting, default 0.001 max_iterations Maximum iterations binary search, default 25 optim_method using optim, method: "Brent", "L-BFGS-B", etc. lower Lower bound p_value search (proportion Cmax), default 0.01 upper Upper bound p_value search (proportion Cmax). Biologically, p = 1.0 maximum ration; values > 1.0 super-maximal. Default 1.0 bootstrap, 5.0 binary_search. hessian Whether compute Hessian standard errors, default FALSE verbose Whether show progress messages, default FALSE confidence_level Confidence level MLE/bootstrap intervals, default 0.95 estimate_sigma Whether estimate measurement error MLE, default TRUE compute_profile Whether compute likelihood profile MLE, default FALSE profile_grid_size Number points profile grid MLE, default 50 n_bootstrap Number bootstrap iterations, default 1000 parallel Whether use parallel processing bootstrap, default FALSE n_cores Number cores parallel processing (NULL = auto-detect) sample_size Sample size bootstrap iteration (NULL = original) compute_percentiles Whether compute additional percentiles bootstrap, default TRUE ... Additional arguments passed strategy-specific functions (e.g., store_predicted_weights_boot bootstrap)

title: Run FB4 simulation on Bioenergetic object — run_fb4.Bioenergetic
text: object class fb4_result, named list four   elements: summary Named list method, p_estimate,       final_weight, total_consumption, method-specific       fields (p_mean, p_sd, confidence intervals MLE       bootstrap, etc.). daily_output data.frame one row per simulation       day containing Day, Weight, Consumption_energy,       Respiration, Egestion, Excretion, SDA,       Net_energy, Energy_density, related columns. method_data Method-specific auxiliary data: bootstrap       p-value distributions percentiles, MLE likelihood profile,       hierarchical population parameters, depending       strategy. bioenergetic_object original Bioenergetic object       x supplied caller.

title: Run FB4 simulation on Bioenergetic object — run_fb4.Bioenergetic
code: # \donttest{ data(fish4_parameters) sp   <- fish4_parameters[["Oncorhynchus tshawytscha"]]$life_stages$adult info <- fish4_parameters[["Oncorhynchus tshawytscha"]]$species_info bio  <- Bioenergetic(   species_params     = sp,   species_info       = info,   environmental_data = list(     temperature = data.frame(Day = 1:30, Temperature = rep(12, 30))   ),   diet_data = list(     proportions = data.frame(Day = 1:30, Prey1 = 1.0),     energies    = data.frame(Day = 1:30, Prey1 = 5000),     prey_names  = "Prey1"   ),   simulation_settings = list(initial_weight = 100, duration = 30) ) #> Bioenergetic object created for: Oncorhynchus tshawytscha bio$species_params$predator$ED_ini <- 5000 bio$species_params$predator$ED_end <- 5500 result <- run_fb4(bio, strategy = "direct", p_value = 0.5, verbose = FALSE) #> Validation warnings: #> Missing optional parameters (will be calculated): CG1, CG2 #> Object is ready for simulation #> Validation warnings: #> Missing optional parameters (will be calculated): CG1, CG2 #> Object is ready for simulation #> Processing species parameters... #> Processing temporal data... #> No indigestible fraction data provided, using default 0% for all prey (FB4 default) #> Processing simulation settings... #> Simulation data preparation complete. Ready for simulation. result$summary$final_weight #> [1] 134.0913 # }

Net change weight (g). relative_growth Numeric. Relative growth percentage       initial weight. total_consumption_g Numeric. Cumulative consumption       simulated days (g prey). total_consumption Numeric. Alias total_consumption_g       (retained backward compatibility). simulation_days Integer. Number days actually simulated;       may less full duration mortality occurs. method List. consumption_method supplied       caller. simulation_completed Logical. TRUE simulation ran       requested days without early termination. mortality_occurred Logical. TRUE fish weight fell       0.01 g simulation. daily_output data.frame one row per simulated day       containing temperature, consumption, respiration, egestion, excretion,       net energy, weight, energy density. present       output_daily = TRUE.

what: Simulation Engine for FB4 Model — simulation-engine
title: Simulation Engine for FB4 Model — simulation-engine
text: Core daily simulation loop Fish Bioenergetics 4.0 model. main entry point run_fb4_simulation, iterates simulated day calling low-level helpers consumption (calculate_daily_consumption), metabolic losses (calculate_daily_metabolism), spawning costs (calculate_daily_spawn_energy), weight change (calculate_daily_weight_change). Consumption can specified proportion maximum (p_value), percentage body weight (ration_percent), absolute grams per day (ration_grams).

what: Binary Search Strategy for FB4 Model — strategy-binary-search
title: Binary Search Strategy for FB4 Model — strategy-binary-search
text: Implements "binary_search" FB4 fitting strategy, finds proportion maximum consumption (p-value) minimises difference simulated metric user-supplied target. Supported fitting targets "Weight" (final weight g) "Consumption" (total consumption g). strategy instantiated create_binary_search_strategy; search algorithm binary_search_p_value, coordinated fit_fb4_binary_search.

what: Bootstrap Estimation Strategy for FB4 Model — strategy-bootstrap
title: Bootstrap Estimation Strategy for FB4 Model — strategy-bootstrap
text: Implements "bootstrap" FB4 fitting strategy, estimates proportion maximum consumption (p-value) uncertainty resampling observed final weights. bootstrap replicate finds p_value minimises difference simulated final weight resampled mean weight via optim_search_p_value. Parallel execution supported future/furrr ecosystem (parallel = TRUE).

what: Strategy Commons for FB4 Model — strategy-commons
title: Strategy Commons for FB4 Model — strategy-commons

Provides unified simulation executor (execute_simulation_with_method), common parameter extractor (extract_strategy_parameters), metadata helpers (add_strategy_metadata), final-simulation runner (run_final_simulation), input validation (validate_common_strategy_inputs), standardised error-result constructor (create_error_result).

what: Direct Strategies for FB4 Model — strategy-direct
title: Direct Strategies for FB4 Model — strategy-direct
text: Implements "direct" FB4 execution strategy, runs single simulation user-supplied value instead searching optimal parameter. Three input modes supported: "p_value" (proportion maximum consumption, 0–5), "ration_percent" (percentage body weight per day, 0–100), "ration_grams" (absolute daily ration grams). strategy instantiated create_direct_strategy executed run_fb4_direct_method.

what: Hierarchical Estimation Strategy for FB4 Model — strategy-hierarchical
title: Hierarchical Estimation Strategy for FB4 Model — strategy-hierarchical
text: Implements "hierarchical" FB4 fitting strategy, estimates population-level individual-level p-values using hierarchical mixed-effects model compiled TMB. Individual p_values treated random effects (log_p_individual), population mean (mu_p) standard deviation (sigma_p) fixed-effect hyperparameters. Optional covariates can supplied explain variation mu_p across individuals.

what: Strategy Interface and Factory for FB4 Model — strategy-interface
title: Strategy Interface and Factory for FB4 Model — strategy-interface
text: Defines common interface FB4 execution strategies must implement (FB4Strategy) provides factory function (create_fb4_strategy) instantiates correct strategy object based requested method. execution plan first assembled create_execution_plan, validated fit_to/strategy concordance validate_fit_to_strategy_concordance, passed strategy's execute() method. Supported strategies "binary_search", "optim", "mle", "bootstrap", "hierarchical", "direct" (ration variants).

title: Maximum Likelihood Estimation Strategy for FB4 Model — strategy-mle

R backend (fit_fb4_mle) faster TMB/C++ backend (execute_mle_tmb) supported. Confidence intervals derived Hessian (delta method) optionally likelihood profile (compute_likelihood_profile).

title: FB4 Uncertainty Propagation Functions — uncertainty-prediction
text: Functions propagating p-value uncertainty consumption predictions using two approaches: delta method (predict_consumption_delta), applies first-order Taylor series approximation efficient consumption-p relationship approximately linear; parametric bootstrap (predict_consumption_bootstrap), samples estimated p-value distribution, runs full FB4 simulation sample, derives full consumption uncertainty distribution without linearity assumptions.

fb4package 2.1.0 (2026-06-06)
text: CRAN release: 2026-06-09

title: fb4package 2.1.0 (2026-06-06)
text: Nutrient sub-model now active — Bioenergetic() accepts nutrient_data (N P concentrations, assimilation efficiencies). supplied, run_fb4() computes daily nitrogen phosphorus fluxes (consumed, growth, excretion, egestion, N:P ratios) appends daily_output. Contaminant sub-model now active — Bioenergetic() accepts contaminant_data. three CONTEQ equations (pure accumulation, T/W-dependent elimination, Arnot & Gobas 2004) now computed within daily loop returned Contaminant_* columns daily_output. Expanded daily_output — three columns added: Starting_Weight (body weight start day), Mean_Prey_Energy_J_g (energy-weighted mean prey energy density), per-prey consumption columns (Cons_<prey>_g) diet item. Validation vignette — new article “Validation FB4-Shiny” (vignettes/fb4-shiny-validation.Rmd) verifies numerical equivalence FB4-Shiny v1.1.7 across five simulation modes nutrient contaminant sub-models.

title: fb4package 2.1.0 (2026-06-06)
text: strategy = "direct" now correctly infers concrete sub-strategy fit_to: "Ration_prey" routes direct_ration_grams "Ration" routes direct_ration_percent, instead always defaulting direct_p_value (#strategy-interface). TMB C++ engine (src/fb4_main.cpp): total consumption, respiration, waste fluxes now accumulated using start--day weight, consistent R simulation engine. extract_hierarchical_parameters(): betas_se longer overwritten already populated; uses NA_real_ instead bare NA.

title: fb4package 2.0.0 (2026-03-28)
text: CRAN release: 2026-05-07 major rewrite. entire codebase reorganised monolithic structure (7 files) modular, strategy-based architecture (33 files), optional TMB/C++ backend added statistical estimation methods.

title: fb4package 2.0.0 (2026-03-28)
text: Added optional TMB (Template Model Builder) backend maximum likelihood hierarchical estimation, providing 10–50× speedup pure-R implementation (src/fb4_main.cpp). New C++ headers consumption, respiration, egestion/excretion, body composition, validation (src/includes/). Added Rcpp, TMB (>= 1.7.0), RcppEigen dependencies. R (>= 4.0.0) now required (>= 3.5.0).

title: fb4package 2.0.0 (2026-03-28)
text: "optim" — gradient-based fitting stats::optim(). "mle" — maximum likelihood estimation p-value SE 95% CI, using observed final weights (observed_weights). "bootstrap" — non-parametric uncertainty quantification via resampling observed final weights. Supports parallel execution (future/furrr). "hierarchical" — population-level mixed-effects model estimating mean SD p across individuals (TMB required).

title: fb4package 2.0.0 (2026-03-28)
text: Simulation engine extracted R/12-simulation-engine.R. Fitting algorithms split individual strategy files (14.2.1 14.2.6), shared commons layer (14.0.1-strategy-commons.R), strategy interface (14.1). Validation split four specialised modules (11.0-core, 11.1-basic, 11.2-parameter, 11.3-data, 11.4-main). Bioenergetic class methods separated 13.0-bioenergetic-classes.R 13.1-bioenergetic-methods.R. Analysis layer split five focused modules (15.0–15.4). Plot layer split four modules (16.1–16.4) covering core helpers, daily output plots, analysis plots, bioenergetic-object plots. Result builders centralised 14.3-result-builders.R consistent field names (p_value, total_consumption_g, converged) across strategies.

title: fb4package 2.0.0 (2026-03-28)
text: analyze_growth_patterns(), analyze_feeding_performance(), analyze_energy_budget(), analyze_growth_temperature_sensitivity(), analyze_population_variation(), comprehensive_nutritional_analysis().

title: fb4package 2.0.0 (2026-03-28)
text: plot.fb4_result(type = "growth" | "consumption" | "temperature" | "energy" | "uncertainty" | "dashboard") plot.Bioenergetic(type = "temperature" | "diet" | "energy_density" | "sensitivity").

title: fb4package 2.0.0 (2026-03-28)
text: R/00-utils.R (v0.1.0) replaced R/utils.R additional helpers (z_score(), safe_exp(), safe_sqrt(), clamp(), %||%). R/09-fitting-algorithms.R replaced strategy layer (14.x). R/10-main-growth-model.R R/12-fb4-main.R replaced R/12-simulation-engine.R R/14.0-run-fb4-orchestrator.R. R/13-visualization-analysis.R split 15.x (analysis) 16.x (plots). R/14-bioenergetic-classes.R split 13.0 13.1.

title: fb4package 0.1.0
text: Initial release. Pure-R implementation Fish Bioenergetics 4.0 (Deslauriers et al. 2017). Binary search fitting (strategy = "binary_search") direct p-value simulation (strategy = "direct"). Bioenergetic S3 class print(), summary(), plot(), run_fb4() methods. Species parameter database 105 models (73 species). compiled code; depends base R packages.
